news_summary: The US government announced February 24, 2023 trade and sanctions actions targeting Russian metals. A key measure imposed 200% duties on Russian aluminum and aluminum-containing products, with additional higher tariffs on other Russian metal products. OFAC also expanded sanctions affecting Russia’s mining and metals sector. | CME Group said Aluminum futures saw record average daily volume in February 2023 and record average daily open interest, along with rising unique users, extending through December 2024. The announcement emphasizes market liquidity and participation growth rather than any change in aluminum supply, demand, or inventories.

The article also says CBP is increasing UFLPA enforcement for aluminum, issuing detention notices, potentially reducing available U.S. supply and pressuring prices lower. | SMM reports that aluminium extrusion scrap prices in Foshan, a major Chinese scrap trading market, rose to 13,600–14,450 yuan/mt on March 3, up 25 yuan/mt from the prior day. The move suggests firmer secondary aluminium scrap valuation that can support nearby aluminium price sentiment. | SMM reported Shanghai A00 aluminium prices at 18,530–18,570 yuan/mt on March 3, up 20 yuan/mt from the prior day. The update reflects a modest day-over-day rise in the tracked aluminium benchmark, pointing to slightly stronger near-term aluminium pricing. | London copper was set for a weekly gain as stronger-than-expected China manufacturing and services data boosted demand sentiment, while declining SHFE inventories suggested improving tightness. Markets also weighed a firmer dollar and potential additional U.S. sanctions on China. Aluminium rose slightly; nickel fell on the day. | SMM reports that two rounds of power rationing in Yunnan cut operating aluminum capacity by more than 40%. Current operating capacity is 3.26 million mt versus 3.37 million mt a year earlier. The curtailments imply lower local supply and tighter aluminum availability in the near term. | Yunnan’s electricity authority warns the province will still face power shortages and uncertainty in 2023. Aluminium smelters there cannot run at full capacity due to power rationing, with actual power consumption and production likely far below expected levels, implying tighter aluminium supply in the near term. | Adani Enterprises said its subsidiary, Mundra Aluminium Ltd, was named preferred bidder for the Ballada bauxite block in Odisha. The LOI covers a large bauxite resource and positions the company for bauxite/alumina processing and aluminium operations, potentially supporting near-term aluminium supply expectations. | Slovenian producer Talum said it will stop primary aluminum production in April because high electricity prices make smelting unprofitable. It will continue downstream aluminum products and may restart later if conditions improve. The move also implies job cuts and output reduction from primary operations. | Slovenian primary aluminium producer Talum says it will halt primary output in April because high electricity prices make production unprofitable. The company already runs only 11 of 160 cells and has been cutting output. The planned curtailment reduces near-term aluminium supply, likely supporting prices.

news_summary: SMM’s Mar 6 commentary says SHFE 2304 aluminium and LME aluminium saw small moves, with fears of further US/Europe rate hikes weighing on commodities. Offsetting this, China’s economic recovery and expectations of supportive policies around the Two Sessions are expected to keep aluminium prices rangebound in the short term. | SMM reports that SHFE’s most-traded April 2023 aluminium contract (2304) rose to close at 18,775 yuan/mt for the week, up 140 yuan/mt. LME aluminium also increased, trading at $2,417.5/mt as of Friday, up $73.5/mt or 3.14% on the week. | SMM’s Mar 6-10, 2023 forecast for the most-traded SHFE aluminium contract (and SMM A00 average) projects prices fluctuating upward or forming an inverted V pattern. Machine-learning ranges and technical indicators show more bullish than bearish signals, with resistance near 18,800–19,100 and support around 18,200–18,300. | SMM reports that fears of further interest rate hikes in Europe and the US may weigh on commodity prices, including aluminium. Offsetting factors include China’s economic recovery and expectations of favorable policy signals around the “Two Sessions.” Overall, SHFE and LME aluminium are seen trading in a wide but rangebound band. | The US announced a 200% tariff on aluminium imports from Russia and on products containing Russian-sourced aluminium, effective March 10/April 10. Following the news, traders rushed to sell Russian holdings, pushing the LME cash price down sharply and widening cash–3-month contango, indicating near-term bearish pressure on aluminium. | SMM reported that Shanghai’s SMM A00 aluminium prices were 18,450–18,490 yuan/mt on Mar 6, down 80 yuan/mt from the prior Friday. The update is a direct spot-price movement for aluminium, signaling near-term bearish pressure for the metal. | SMM says China’s social aluminium ingot inventories held at 1.27 million mt as of March 6, flat on the week but up versus late January and last year. Warehouse outflows increased slightly and some storage capacity constraints appeared, while in-transit volumes declined. Aluminium billet inventories rose marginally, with lower billet outflows. | The US may face difficulties enforcing provenance checks for Russian aluminium under a proposed 200% tariff, but Russian volumes into the US are small. LME warranting of aluminium in US warehouses was suspended and premiums rose slightly, suggesting limited but supportive pricing pressure for aluminium. | Copper fell 1.5% on Monday after disappointment with China’s ~5% growth target hurt sentiment for industrial metals. Traders also pointed to rising LME inventories and attention ahead of Powell testimony and U.S. non-farm payrolls, which could shift rate and dollar expectations. Aluminium also declined. | Alcoa is seeking approval for expanded bauxite mining and exploration near Perth’s Serpentine Dam in jarrah forest, amid government concerns that runoff from cleared slopes could damage drinking water quality. The WA EPA has 28 days to decide whether to investigate, raising the risk of operational constraints and supply delays. | Human Rights Watch highlights claims from Bloomberg that Ford’s aluminum supply chain is linked to deforestation and harm in Brazil’s Amazon, involving bauxite mining and refining impacts on land, water, and communities. It also notes past forced-labor allegations in China tied to a U.S. import ban, raising compliance risk for aluminum.

news_summary: Queensland will take control of a $5bn, 1,100km high-voltage CopperString transmission line linking Mount Isa to the national grid. The government says it will enable development of “critical minerals” and large renewable generation, with construction expected to begin next year. Costs have risen and state-owned transmission will build it. | SMM reports that Shanghai A00 aluminium prices were 18,550–18,590 yuan/mt on March 7, up 100 yuan/mt from the previous day. The note is a market price update, suggesting near-term bullish momentum for aluminium. | Copper prices edged higher as the dollar weakened and traders anticipated improving China demand from March. LME three-month copper rose slightly, and Shanghai April copper also increased. Aluminium eased on the day and nickel declined, indicating the bullish impulse was strongest for copper rather than industrial metals broadly. | SIG says it will source 100% of aluminium used in its aseptic carton packs from January 2023 under Aluminium Stewardship Initiative (ASI) standards worldwide, allowing customers to display an ASI label. The company frames the move as lower-carbon, responsible sourcing, but provides no volume, supply disruption, or pricing details. | Aluminum Dynamics will begin construction on a $2.5 billion aluminium mill in Mississippi, with Steel Dynamics as owner. The project includes co-locating customers and plans for 1,000 jobs over seven years. It signals future aluminium supply capacity but lacks timing or production ramp details, limiting short-term price impact. | Gulbrandsen inaugurated a new facility in Dahej, India, beginning production of triethylaluminum (TEAL). The company says the move is to meet rising global demand for aluminum alkyls used as co-catalysts in polyolefins, synthetic rubber, pharmaceuticals and other fine chemicals, supporting incremental aluminum-linked demand.

It also projects a copper shortage turning to surplus in 2024-25. | SMM reported that ADC12 secondary aluminium alloy prices in Shanghai were 19,500–19,700 yuan/mt on March 8, down 100 yuan/mt from the prior day. The note is a market price update for secondary aluminium alloy, indicating short-term bearish pricing pressure for aluminium products. | Huatai Securities expects China’s recovery to improve copper and aluminium supply-demand conditions. Downstream processing enterprise operating rates should rise from March to April. Tighter refined aluminium production restrictions are flagged as an important 2023 market driver, while the copper shortage expected in 2024-2025 could turn into surplus over the next two years. | Speira will fully ramp down aluminium smelting at Rheinwerk, ending remaining 70,000 tonnes of liquid aluminium output in 2H 2023, citing still-high power costs. The firm will expand recycling instead. LME aluminium has fallen 37% over the past year, with demand concerns lingering despite fewer sanctions and easing China restrictions. | After four weeks of declining, China’s domestic aluminium billet social inventory rose by 7,200 mt to 163,400 mt by Mar 9. Higher molten output and eased storage restrictions boosted arrivals, while demand recovery lagged. news_summary: German metal processor Speira will close its aluminium smelting operations at the Rheinwerk plant because energy prices are too high. It previously cut production by 50% in 2022 and now plans to shut the remaining 70,000t of liquid aluminium output in 2H23, shifting toward rolling and recycling. | Canada has introduced sanctions that ban imports of Russian steel and aluminium. By restricting Russian aluminium inflows, the policy may tighten supply available to Canadian buyers and improve near-term market sentiment for aluminium prices, depending on how quickly alternative sources can replace the volumes. | Canada banned imports of all Russian aluminum (including sheets and containers) and steel products to limit Russian revenue from the war in Ukraine. The move coincides with the US’s 200% tariff on Russian aluminum that took effect on Friday, likely tightening supply and supporting aluminium prices. | Canada bans imports of Russian steel and aluminum to further undermine Russia’s invasion of Ukraine, aiming to weaken Moscow’s war capacity. Officials cite small additional trade exposure: prior tariffs already applied and Russian inflows were limited. The report suggests supply-chain pressure but overall “quite muted” effects; copper impact is indirect and unquantified. | Canada introduced regulations banning imports into Canada (and Canadian purchases abroad) of Russian aluminum and aluminum articles, effective from registration. The measure reduces Russian supply availability for Canadian buyers and may require rerouting trade, potentially tightening regional aluminium markets and supporting prices in the near term. | Canada bans Russian aluminum and steel imports, including finished and unfinished aluminum products, to curb revenue supporting Russia’s invasion of Ukraine. The ban aligns with U.S. action imposing a 200% tariff on Russian aluminum. Canada imported about C$45 million of aluminum from Russia in 2021. | Canada announced a ban on importing Russian steel and aluminum as part of sanctions over the Ukraine invasion. Reported additional curtailed trade is expected to be small because Russian shipments had already declined and imports faced prior tariffs, though the move may add some supply-chain pressure. Copper and nickel are not addressed. | Canada announced a ban on all Russian aluminum (and steel) imports starting March 10 to limit revenue Moscow gains from its war in Ukraine. The measure cuts a direct source of aluminium supply into the Canadian market, likely tightening availability and supporting near-term aluminium prices. | Canada is banning Russian steel and aluminum imports, with the announcement tied to government remarks about Moscow. The aluminum import ban is likely to reduce Russia-origin supply available to Canadian buyers and re-route trade, which can support near-term aluminium price expectations within weeks. | Canada will ban imports of Russian steel and aluminum under its sanctions against Moscow. Foreign Affairs Minister Joly ties the policy to weakening Russia’s ability to attack Ukraine and possible regime change. Industry sources say Russia’s Canadian aluminum/steel volumes are small and the overall commercial impact is likely muted, limiting price effects.

The decline points to improved smelter economics and may support near-term aluminium prices. | Alcoa said its Portland Aluminium joint-venture smelter in Victoria will cut production to about 75% of capacity immediately because of operational instability, including rodded anode production issues. news_summary: Copper held steady to slightly higher in Asia after a deal to buy SVB’s assets improved risk appetite, supporting dollar-priced metals. However, gains were capped by weak Chinese demand tied to overseas macro factors and higher prices. Aluminium and nickel also rose modestly, reflecting the same sentiment move. | Power and water shortages in China’s Yunnan province are disrupting hydroelectric supply, prompting authorities to order aluminum smelters to cut electricity consumption and production, most recently by 40%. Yunnan is a key smelting hub, and repeated curtailments suggest near-term supply tightness likely supportive of aluminum prices.

news_summary: Copper prices edged lower in Asia as investors stayed cautious after U.S. banking turmoil, though fears of a full crisis eased. Nickel slid but was recently supported by falling inventories and short-covering. Aluminium fell. Support from a weaker dollar and mixed Shanghai moves suggest no strong net direction. | LX Hausys and Belgium’s Reynaers Aluminium signed an MoU to cooperate on design, manufacturing technology, and development of high-end aluminium windows for the Korean market. New ultraslim and insulated window products are planned for the second half of this year, supported by rising demand for renovations and premium facilities. | Copper, nickel and aluminium prices fell as investors stayed cautious after banking-sector turmoil. Reuters cites position cuts and risk-off trading by funds and companies, described as short-term deleveraging. Even with a weaker U.S. dollar, LME copper, nickel and aluminium retreated in the session. | Ghana’s Lands and Natural Resources Minister says efforts to establish an integrated aluminium industry via GIADEC are progressing, with plans to develop bauxite supply, build refineries, and modernise/retrofit VALCO. A downstream workshop focuses on policy and incentives to raise local consumption and reduce aluminium imports. | Ghana’s minister and GIADEC highlight plans to build an integrated aluminium downstream industry, leveraging large domestic bauxite resources and AfCFTA market access. They stress policy incentives, including power and tax support, to enable refining and downstream fabrication, which could raise near-term expectations for aluminium production and demand.

news_summary: SMM data for the week ending Mar 31 shows aluminium inventories falling in China’s bonded zones. Shanghai bonded stocks were 50,000 mt (up from 49,300 mt in Guangdong), while combined stocks reached 99,300 mt, down 380 mt versus the prior week, implying slightly tighter supply conditions. | Pampa Metals began a diamond-drill program at its Buenavista target within the Block 4 project in northern Chile, aimed at testing a porphyry copper-molybdenum-gold system. The company expects assay results in June. This is early exploration activity with limited near-term implications for copper prices. | GIADEC and Ghana’s Ministry of Lands and Natural Resources are planning a policy framework for the downstream aluminium industry, emphasizing power costs and tax incentives. The government highlights value addition from bauxite to alumina and primary aluminium, aiming to expand refining and downstream manufacturing. This should be modestly supportive for aluminium market expectations. | GIADEC’s CEO says Ghana is preparing a policy framework to govern the aluminium downstream industry, with incentives and power-cost measures highlighted. The plan aims to expand refining and downstream production to serve sectors like construction, foil and car manufacturing, supported by AfCFTA market access.

news_summary: Copper prices fell on Monday as China’s Caixin manufacturing PMI weakened to the 50.0 level, raising demand concerns. A firmer U.S. dollar tied to renewed inflation worries after oil producers’ output cuts further pressured dollar-priced commodities. LME and SHFE aluminium also eased, while nickel was mixed. | Copper prices eased on a stronger dollar after higher oil prices revived inflation and potential U.S. rate-hike fears. China’s Caixin/S&P manufacturing PMI fell to 50.0, suggesting demand slowdown. LME warehouse stocks declined, offering some support, but copper still traded lower. Aluminium rose on electricity-cost worries.

news_summary: Meridian and NZ Aluminium Smelters agreed to give the Tiwai Point smelter flexibility to reduce electricity demand when the New Zealand grid is under stress. As the smelter uses about 12% of current generation, such demand curtailment implies potential production adjustments that could pressure aluminium prices. | Indonesia’s Inalum has been separated from the MIND ID holding structure and is now focused on aluminium smelting and developing the national aluminium industry. The split follows government regulations transferring controlling shares. While it strengthens governance and planning, the article does not cite near-term changes to aluminium output or supply. | Copper prices fell toward a one-week low on weak U.S. economic data and worries about demand, but a further drop looked limited due to thin liquidity from China’s Qingming holiday (SHFE closed). Analysts cited a potentially stronger demand/d tighter supply outlook, while LME aluminium and nickel were also slightly lower. | New Zealand Aluminium Smelter (NZAS) and Meridian agreed to a demand-response deal allowing Meridian to request up to 50 MW reductions during grid stress or hydro shortages, with Meridian paying fees per reduction. The agreement is subject to Electricity Authority approval and aims to maintain reliable smelter power supply through next winter. | Vedanta said its aluminium production in Q4FY23 rose to 574,000 tonnes, slightly above the prior year. However, alumina output at its Lanjigarh refinery fell 7% QoQ and 18% YoY due to a planned calciner shutdown. The update is specific to aluminium production levels. | Copper prices rose on LME support from falling inventories, lower cancelled warrants, and drawdown dynamics, despite worries over weaker U.S. and China manufacturing and a stronger dollar. It also cites reduced global smelting activity and lower Chile production. Aluminium was reported lower during the session, and macro sentiment weighed broadly. | Copper prices in London rose 0.5% to around $8,790/tonne as LME inventories continued to decline, with warehouse stocks down almost 15% over two weeks and on-warrant stocks at the lowest since late 2021. Aluminium rose modestly amid increasing Russian LME stock share; nickel and other metals were mixed. | Copper prices steadied at a one-week high after China posted stronger-than-expected bank lending and faster credit growth, supported by continued policy measures post-COVID curbs. Rising copper supply expectations tempered gains. The same macro tone lifted LME aluminium and nickel slightly, alongside cautious sentiment ahead of U.S. inflation data. | Copper climbed on Wednesday, helped by a weaker U.S. dollar and stronger-than-expected Chinese credit growth ahead of inflation data. However, gains were tempered by rising refined copper output in China, driven by new capacity and adequate raw-material supply. LME aluminium and nickel also rose alongside copper sentiment. | London copper prices fell from a one-week high as China’s refined output rose 14% year-on-year, increasing supply, while macro-economic pressure weighed on demand expectations. The weaker U.S. dollar offered some support. Aluminium was slightly higher, while nickel traded with a larger swing amid mixed broader base-metals pricing. | Western Copper and Gold said Rio Tinto will exercise its participation right in full, subscribing for 878,809 shares and keeping its ~7.84% stake, alongside Mitsubishi’s larger investment. The company is developing the Casino copper-gold project, implying marginally improved copper project funding visibility near term. | Glencore reportedly added more Russian aluminium to LME-registered warehouses in Gwangyang, lifting Russian stocks’ share and total inventories. Analysts link the build to customer boycotts rather than weaker overall demand. Concern is that larger Russian inventory overhang could weigh on LME benchmark aluminium pricing used in contracts. | Latin Metals Inc. said mapping and prospecting at its Auquis Project in Peru identified two new mineralized zones, including porphyry-style copper and skarn mineralization. news_summary: SMM reports SHFE and LME aluminium prices rising after softer-than-expected US inflation weakened expectations for May rate hikes and a weaker US dollar. Domestically, aluminium supply is only slightly higher and downstream demand is recovering slowly, so the market is seen as rangebound, with Yunnan power supply and inventories in focus. | SMM reports continued declines in aluminium fluoride prices, with April bids cut to 8,900 yuan/mt and March 11–April 10 seeing further falls. While some aluminium smelters in Sichuan and Guizhou are resuming production, demand support is limited. Abundant supply may pressure prices further in the near term, with uncertain spillover to aluminium. | SMM reports that aluminium scrap prices rose in March but weak demand capped further gains. Aluminium extrusion scrap increased by 300–500 yuan/mt and aluminium tense scrap by 100–300 yuan/mt. With both supply and demand growing, aluminium scrap prices are expected to remain rangebound in April. | China’s Jan–March unwrought aluminium and product exports totaled about 1.4 million tonnes, down 15.4% versus the prior year, according to customs data cited by Reuters. The drop suggests weaker export flows, potentially weighing on near-term aluminium market sentiment and pricing. | China imported 408,174 tonnes of unwrought copper and copper products in March, down 19% from a year earlier, according to customs data. The same report notes aluminium exports of 497,412 tonnes in March. Copper demand expectations may soften, while aluminium impact is less definitive without fuller trade context. | SMM says aluminium ingot inventories fell to about 1 million mt as of April 10, down over 200,000 mt from early March. Downstream production resumption boosted procurement. Aluminium plants increased liquid proportion to a March high of 71%, reducing ingot output. Inventories are expected to keep declining in April, per the SMM ML model. | Aluminum prices have been rangebound after a February decline, with March support and a mild upward bias into April. China’s imports of Russian aluminum nearly tripled in early 2023, offsetting Western trade restrictions amid China’s slow demand from water shortages. news_summary: Benchmark LME copper edged up on a softer U.S. dollar, but traders stayed cautious amid concerns over China’s lacklustre demand and uncertainty ahead of the Fed meeting. Meanwhile, LME aluminium fell and nickel dropped more sharply, highlighting mixed momentum across industrial metals. | Vedanta Aluminium unveiled a new material recovery facility at its Jharsuguda site to support a circular economy by improving recovery of materials for reuse/recycling. The project is expected to increase the availability of recycled inputs over time, which may modestly soften near-term aluminium price expectations. | Copper prices were subdued in Asian trade amid concerns over weak China demand and caution ahead of upcoming U.S. economic data and the Fed meeting. A softer U.S. dollar offered some support. LME aluminium and nickel fell on the day, while Shanghai showed mixed moves, reflecting choppy near-term sentiment. | Century Aluminum Company announced plans to acquire a 55% interest in the Jamalco alumina refinery. news_summary: Alcoa said it reached a tentative three-year labor agreement with the United Steelworkers for workers at its Warrick (Indiana) and Massena (New York) aluminum smelters. The pact covers about 860 employees and will be voted on by union members, with the prior contract expiring May 15, 2023. | Pan Global said it has secured surface access to begin drilling immediately at the Romana West target within its Escacena project in Spain, aiming to test an on-strike extension of the La Romana copper-tin-silver discovery. Two rigs will mobilize and 25–30 holes are planned, with results pending. | Credit Suisse upgraded Alcoa, saying the stock could gain over 35% as LME aluminum prices rebound. The note cites recovering global demand, supportive U.S./EU physical premiums, improving Chinese outlook, and supply dynamics tied to hydro challenges and an expected Spain smelter restart, despite noted risks.

news_summary: Macro sentiment turned bearish, driven by renewed expectations of US rate hikes, banking crisis worries, and debt-ceiling negotiations. SMM reports aluminium ingot inventories falling, but downstream operating rates remain mediocre and billet inventories rise. Smelter output and operating rates increased in April, with further growth expected in May. | China’s aluminium billet social inventory reached 168,600 mt as of May 11, up 10,900 mt from a week earlier. SMM attributes the build to continued cargo arrivals, weak downstream demand, and more molten aluminium being converted into billets. With extrusion moving into off-season, billet inventories face further accumulation risk. | SMM reports China aluminium ingot social inventories at 787,000 mt as of May 11, down 48,000 mt week-on-week and 216,000 mt year-on-year. Destocking remains fast and stocks are near five-year lows. Lower premium levels and fading arrivals are driving withdrawals, suggesting inventories stay low near term. | SMM reports SHFE 2306 aluminium ended down 1.21% and LME aluminium fell 2.49%. It notes improving conversion of molten aluminium into billets could affect end-demand transmission to inventories. If demand remains weak, smelters may shift to more ingot output, but low ingot inventories may limit downside. Watches macro and Yunnan supply. | Copper was up on Friday but set for its biggest weekly drop since November as weak global economic data darkened demand expectations and rising supplies pressured prices. LME copper inventories rose toward a two-month high and a stronger dollar hurt sentiment, weighing on the broader metals complex including nickel and aluminium. | Copper hit the lowest level in over five months as fears of slowing demand in China, rising LME warehouse inventories, and a stronger U.S. dollar pressured prices. The sell-off was reinforced by recent Chinese inflation data. Aluminium was slightly higher on the day, while nickel’s gains reflected short-covering amid expected surpluses. | Norilsk Nickel said Rotterdam has allowed it to continue selling nickel through its transshipment hub after earlier disruptions. The company is also using the Tangier port in Morocco for product logistics. Continued nickel deliveries point to easing supply constraints, likely supporting nickel prices in the coming days.

news_summary: UK Prime Minister Rishi Sunak announced an embargo on imports of Russian diamonds and also copper, aluminium and nickel. The measure is part of sanctions related to Russia’s actions in Ukraine, alongside asset freezes and individual/entity restrictions. The EU is also considering diamond trade restrictions. | Copper is set for a fifth straight weekly decline as the U.S. dollar stays near two-month highs and markets price in additional Fed rate hikes. Weak Chinese demand dampens sentiment. Aluminium and nickel show intraday gains, but the broader macro backdrop caps industrial metals, leaving weekly momentum bearish. | Ahead of the G7 Japan summit, the UK is set to unveil sanctions and export controls targeting Russia. Trade Data Monitor shows Britain imported little or no Russian copper, but did import unwrought aluminium and refined nickel from Russia in 2022-2023. Such curbs can reduce Russian metal flows and affect short-term pricing expectations. | Britain and other G7 states plan new sanctions and export controls against Russia. It notes some Russian-origin refined copper was imported previously (none in 2021/2022 refined), aluminium supply from Russia, and Russian refined nickel. Curbs on such flows can tighten supply and lift prices. | Lindian Resources signed a six-year bauxite supply agreement to deliver 23 million wet metric tonnes from Guinea’s Gaoual project to C&D Logistics. The contract specifies alumina- and silica-quality limits and follows an Indonesia bauxite export ban, which the company says increased demand for Guinea bauxite. | UK Prime Minister Sunak plans new import bans on Russian copper, aluminium and nickel as part of fresh G7 sanctions. The factbox details limited UK prior imports from Russia (nickel none in early 2023; aluminium and copper past volumes). Reduced Russian trade is likely to tighten supply and support prices. | The UK said it will announce new import bans on Russian copper, aluminium and nickel alongside fresh G7 sanctions at a summit in Japan. Trade data show the UK imported limited Russian volumes—none for refined copper, none early-2023 for aluminium and nickel—while refined nickel and unwrought aluminium imports occurred in 2022. | The UK plans new import bans on Russian copper, aluminium and nickel, alongside new G7 sanctions targeting Russia over the war in Ukraine. Britain imported no Russian refined copper in recent years, while it bought unwrought aluminium and some refined nickel from Russia in 2022. The bans could tighten supply expectations. | Copper rebounded 1.2% on LME on optimism that U.S. debt talks will avert a default, lifting broader risk assets and supported by a slightly weaker dollar. The article also notes gains in aluminium and a strong rise in nickel, reflecting macro-driven sentiment despite headwinds from China demand and recession risks. | Copper rebounded as investors grew more optimistic that U.S. debt talks will avert a federal default. The piece also flags headwinds from weak China demand and recession risks ahead.

news_summary: SMM says overseas alumina prices fell $5/mt to $340/mt FOB Western Australia last week, or about 2,974 yuan/mt CIF major Chinese ports after freight. The yuan impact is adjusted by ocean freight, with the article noting the US dollar strengthened and the import window has been closed since Jan. 18. | SMM reports aluminium prices have weakened in recent trading. It cites macro headwinds from prolonged US debt-ceiling talks increasing market uncertainty and notes fundamentals remain soft: end-user consumption is only recovering while the market is oversupplied. Lower prebaked anode and coal prices reduce cost support, increasing downside risk. | Vietnam is reported to have the world’s second-largest bauxite reserves (5.8 billion tonnes). Bauxite is refined into alumina and then smelted into aluminium. The province of Dak Nong plans to expand alumina and aluminium smelting, positioning itself as a bauxite-to-aluminium hub, but the article doesn’t cite near-term output changes. | BNN Bloomberg reports Russia is raising aluminum exports to China to near-record levels, attributing the move to sanctions tightening other trade routes. The larger flow into a major consuming region is likely to increase near-term supply expectations and weigh on aluminum prices over the next several days to weeks. | Reuters reports copper fell on deteriorating demand expectations in China, alongside rising supplies and climbing LME stocks (up 80% since mid-April). Mine resumptions in Peru and a DRC royalty agreement supporting copper exports eased supply worries, widening the cash discount. Other metals were mixed; aluminum slid and nickel edged up. | Novelis announced the startup of a new roll forming development line in Michigan to accelerate R&D and enable large-volume production of high-strength aluminum automotive parts. The company says the technology supports electrification and demand for lighter, sustainable components, potentially supporting higher aluminum usage expectations.

news_summary: Congo plans to boost its stake and control in the Sicomines infrastructure-for-minerals deal with China, seeking higher equity for Gecamines, a larger infrastructure loan envelope, and lump-sum compensation over alleged undervaluation of copper and cobalt sales. Negotiations with Chinese firms are expected to intensify after Tshisekedi returns from China.

Japan and South Korea absorbed over 70% of April shipments (mainly via tolling), implying reduced export supply to key buyers. | SMM reports SHFE 2307 aluminium opened around 17,750 yuan/mt and LME aluminium around $2,209/mt, both up modestly. news_summary: Indonesian copper miner Amman Mineral’s $880m IPO will finance debt repayment and projects, including completing a copper smelter in Sumbawa with planned cathode output. The Indonesian government’s temporary raw export exemption supports revenue needed to finish the project, reinforcing near-term copper sentiment. | Copper prices slid after China’s manufacturing PMI came in weaker than expected, below 50 indicating contraction, reinforcing concerns about demand in the world’s top consumer. Copper was set for a second monthly loss and fell 5.8% so far this month. LME aluminium and nickel also declined alongside risk sentiment. | ALRO signed an MoU with Azeraluminium to explore cooperation on raw material supply (baked anodes and alumina), aluminum product sales, and processing/marketing of continuous casting coils. The parties also discuss developing a gas-fired power plant on ALRO’s premises, aiming to improve technical and economic capabilities. | Copper prices edged toward six-month lows after May China factory activity contracted faster than expected, reinforcing concerns that the rebound is losing steam. Japan and South Korea output also fell. A weaker yuan pressured dollar-priced metals, while physical premiums rose and LME inventories increased. | SMM’s May 31 market wrap says crude oil slipped and the U.S. dollar rose, with supply-side headlines around Iran/OPEC. Base metals mostly fell: SHFE nickel plunged 4.81%, SHFE copper was slightly down, and SHFE aluminium rose modestly. LME metals were largely lower, with aluminium nearly flat. | BHP’s Escondida in Chile will use AI and Azure-based analytics on real-time concentrator data to improve ore processing and grade recovery. The initiative aims to extract more value from existing resources as copper is expected to face future supply shortfalls from green energy and battery demand. | LME launched two consultations to reform market rules after last year’s nickel crisis, including expanded warehouse reporting on off-warrant inventories and changes to how closing prices are set. The VWAP-based closing method would include more aluminium, copper and nickel contracts. Timing is June–July, not immediate rule changes.

news_summary: China’s net imports of primary aluminium in Q1 2023 rose to 213,000 mt, up 804% year-on-year, with Russia supplying 172,500 mt. Russian producers expect sustained demand from Chinese downstream users for low-carbon aluminium. The article implies China will rely heavily on Russia going forward, supporting aluminium sentiment. | Altemira outlines its integrated aluminum can-to-can recycling system: used beverage cans are collected, melted and processed by group entities to make new cans, using UBC scrap instead of primary ingots and cutting CO2 by about 40%. The company cites Japan’s ~70% can recycling rates and expects carbon-neutral policy updates. | Copper prices slipped after China reported weaker-than-expected May exports and contracted imports of unwrought copper and products, signaling softer global demand. A weaker U.S. dollar and hopes for additional Chinese property-sector stimulus supported prices. Analysts also expect refined copper tightness to ease as smelters restart production and port stocks rise. Aluminium rose modestly. | Aluminium Bahrain’s chairman discussed the firm’s decarbonisation efforts at an Arab-German business forum, highlighting Power Station 5 Block 4 and solar farm projects to reduce greenhouse-gas intensity, alongside reforestation initiatives. The message supports Bahrain’s net-zero target but does not indicate immediate changes to aluminium output or sales impacting near-term prices. | U.S. Trade Representative Katherine Tai met France’s Bruno Le Maire and said she is hopeful the U.S. and EU can reach an agreement this year on steel and aluminum trade. A deal could reduce trade friction and tariffs, likely improving near-term expectations for aluminum supply-demand and supporting prices.

news_summary: Indonesia is set to ban bauxite exports from Saturday, aiming to boost domestic alumina refining. Industry groups argue existing refinery capacity (four smelters) can’t absorb output (more than 27 million tonnes), potentially forcing producers to cut output or delay shipments. The government says it will evaluate impacts after implementation. | SMM reports the weighted alumina index at 2,843 yuan/mt, down just 1 yuan/mt week-on-week. The key near-term driver is whether aluminium smelters in Yunnan can restart amid unstable hydropower. Partial restart could leave alumina supply excessive, pressuring prices further; full restart could absorb surplus and stabilize. | SMM’s daily comments note three straight days of crude oil declines and a largely flat U.S. dollar. At intraday close, SHFE copper was down 0.07% and aluminium down 0.87%, while SHFE nickel rose 2.22%. Other metals also moved, but the key tracked effects are mixed. | Russian aluminium registered with London Metal Exchange warehouses rose to 68% of total holdings in May (from 52% in April), with 263,125 tonnes in warehouses. The LME said Russian metal continues to flow and be accepted. Concentrated stocks raise concerns about price distortion, likely weighing on aluminium sentiment. | Rio Tinto will invest $1.1 billion to expand its AP60 low-carbon aluminium smelter in Quebec, adding 96 new AP60 pots and about 160,000 tonnes of primary aluminium capacity per year. The project offsets closures at Arvida and aims to meet demand for low-carbon aluminium, with commissioning starting in H1 2026. | Rio Tinto will invest $1.1 billion to expand its low-carbon aluminium smelter at Complexe Jonquiere, Canada, adding about 160,000 tonnes/year and offsetting capacity lost from potroom closures. The project runs over two-and-a-half years and fully ramps by end-2026, supported partly by Quebec. | Rio Tinto will invest $1.1 billion to expand its low-carbon aluminium smelter at Complexe Jonquière in Quebec, with up to $113 million from Quebec government support. The expansion will align with potroom closures at Arvida. Construction runs 2.5 years, with new pots commissioning in 1H 2026. | Copper fell 0.7% as traders worried about China’s demand outlook and awaited U.S. Fed and inflation data. Earlier gains were linked to covering shorts and Chinese stimulus optimism. Falling LME stocks offer some support, but expectations of elevated rates and China uncertainty weigh on copper and aluminium. | Rio Tinto will invest $1.4 billion to expand its Quebec aluminum smelter by adding 96 AP60 pots, with Quebec funding up to $150 million. The project targets lower greenhouse-gas emissions and is expected to take about 2.5 years to build, boosting aluminum supply capacity. | Canada signed a MoU with Rio Tinto to expand its Complexe Jonquière aluminum smelter by investing CAD $1.4 billion. The project will scale low-carbon AP60 electrolysis and deploy ELYSIS technology, adding new Western smelting capacity and increasing jobs during construction. | Rio Tinto will invest $1.4 billion to expand its Quebec aluminum smelter, adding AP60 pots and raising primary aluminum capacity to about 220,000 tonnes per year. The project is supported by a Quebec loan and includes plans to partially close older lines while adding recycling capacity. | Rio Tinto said it will invest $1.4 billion to expand its aluminium smelter complex in Saguenay, Quebec, adding 96 new pots using low-carbon AP60 technology. The move targets greener aluminum production capacity at the Jonquiére/Arvida site, which may support longer-run supply expectations but is unlikely to strongly move prices immediately. | The Aluminum Extruders Council says U.S. Department of Commerce rules grant tariff-free treatment to foreign extruded aluminum via broad exclusions, while domestic producers face higher proof burdens. It claims untariffed imports rose 82% and penetration exceeded 25%—a record—prompting calls to revoke the General Approved Exclusion process. | Rio Tinto will invest $1.4B to expand its Jonquière aluminum smelter in Quebec with 96 new low-carbon AP60 pots, targeting 220,000 tonnes of primary output and replacing jobs affected by the Arvida closure in 2025. Quebec will provide support contingent on job retention and emissions targets. | Rio Tinto will invest $1.4B to expand its Jonquiere, Quebec aluminium smelter, adding 96 AP60 low-carbon pots and increasing capacity by roughly 220,000 tonnes per year. The project is intended to replace jobs as the older Arvida plant closes in 2025, supported by Quebec funding.

LME says demand remains adequate, despite concerns about price distortion. | A global aluminium producer is reducing its Q3 premium offer to $170/T from an initial $180/T. Japan sets regional benchmarks via premiums paid over the LME cash price, indicating firmer near-term aluminium pricing. | Yunnan’s hydropower shortages previously forced aluminium smelters to cut output by roughly 40%, with curbs reducing capacity by 1.65 million tonnes. The news is exploration-oriented and does not indicate near-term supply changes, so market price impact is likely minimal. | Aston Bay reported new gravity and inversion results at its Storm Copper Project in Nunavut, identifying large, shallow (to ~200m) anomaly targets coincident with historical IP/EM signals and known copper mineralization. A 2022 drill hole intersected ~68.8m of chalcopyrite-bearing sulfides, suggesting potential for a larger copper system. | Golden Arrow reported Phase 1 drill results from its San Pietro IOCG project in Chile, including 34m at 0.41% Cu and high cobalt intercepts, plus additional shallow cobalt targets. It is exploration-focused and does not indicate near-term production changes, so near-term effects on copper prices are likely limited. | MacDonald Mines began a 1,200m drilling program at its Alwyn Cu-Au trend near Sudbury, reporting iron-silicate alteration with chalcopyrite that may indicate iron-rich IOCG-style mineralization. The release is exploratory rather than supply-related, with only limited, indirect relevance to copper and potential nickel. | Indonesia has enforced a bauxite export ban, prompting miners to seek a price floor and more funding for smelter development to avoid large layoffs. With domestic production and smelter capacity mismatched, the ban could reduce bauxite availability, but authorities expect absorption via rising smelter utilization. | Rio Tinto will invest CAD 1.4bn to expand its AP60 aluminium smelter at Jonquière, adding 96 AP60 pots and about 160,000 tonnes of primary aluminium capacity per year, alongside commissioning a recycling facility. The company highlights lower-carbon production supported by Quebec.

Guinea’s rainy season may slightly reduce second-half shipments, but 2023 imports from Guinea are estimated to exceed 90 million mt, up about 30% YoY. More bauxite feedstock is generally supportive for aluminium supply expectations. | Indonesia implemented a bauxite export ban from June 10, 2023, with no bauxite exports to China in April. Some Chinese alumina refineries cut output, while others secured alternative bauxite sources. The ban is expected to keep bauxite prices high, limiting margin relief for refineries and supporting aluminium-linked costs. | SMM reports that Yunnan is likely to issue a power load distribution notice for local aluminium smelters, offering hope for partial restarts of about 2 million mt of idled capacity. Clearer power guidance can quickly influence production expectations and near-term aluminium supply dynamics, supporting short-term price sentiment. | Shanghai copper rallied to a seven-week high as China’s demand outlook improved following a central bank rate cut and as tight supply conditions emerged. Refined output fell on maintenance and import activity weakened. A softer U.S. dollar provided additional support. LME aluminium and nickel also rose. | SECO/WARWICK says two European manufacturers (Poland and the Czech Republic) ordered additional EV/CAB continuous brazing lines for electric-vehicle battery coolers. The company highlights growing EV demand and aluminum brazing furnace usage for mass production, suggesting firmer near-term aluminum heat-exchanger demand. | A Jamaican court decision lifted an injunction that had restricted parts of Noranda/Discovery Bauxite’s mining operations. The company expects bauxite shipments to the Gramercy U.S. alumina refinery to return toward normal levels after a prior decline of about 40% during the legal wait. | SMM’s daily comments say base metals rose across SHFE and mostly higher on LME. It keeps a bearish view on US Midwest aluminium premiums and overall aluminium price bias.

news_summary: SMM reports SHFE and LME aluminium futures closed lower overnight. It attributes pressure to gradually released Yunnan supply, weakening downstream consumption, and rising (though still low) aluminium ingot social inventories in China. Macro uncertainty and potential import inflows are noted, with the short-term forecast suggesting rangebound trading. | London copper rebounded 0.3% as the dollar eased, but gains were capped by uncertainty over Chinese demand and expectations for further U.S. Fed rate hikes. LME aluminium rose 0.6% and nickel edged up slightly, reflecting mixed sentiment across metals tied to broader risk assets. | SMM data show China’s aluminium billet social stocks fell 10,200 mt WoW to 136,100 mt as of July 6, though they remain near a three-year high. Lower billet prices improved demand in Foshan and Wuxi, while smelters and some plants cut output, reducing arrivals. Inventories stay heavy, but supply is easing. | SMM data show China’s eight major markets’ aluminium ingot social stocks rose to 525,000 mt as of July 6, up on-week and above July 3, though still below last year. Increased arrivals and smelters producing ingots, amid off-season demand, pushed spot premiums down quickly and fueled bearish sentiment for July. | Copper edged up but remained in a tight range as investors weighed U.S. Fed rate expectations and a pending jobs report against China demand outlook. China’s property and new-energy activity supported demand, while expanding domestic supply from smelters and rising aluminium output capped gains. Aluminium and nickel also saw modest moves. | Copper rose about 0.6% on Friday as LME-registered warehouse stocks fell to their lowest since April. The market shifted to the upcoming U.S. jobs report, which could influence risk appetite, rate expectations, and the dollar, indirectly affecting copper demand. Aluminium edged up slightly, while other metals fell. | The US agreed to clear 80% of aluminium applications and 70% of steel applications for India-origin products as India and the US resolved six trade disputes outside the WTO. The decision should improve immediate market access and potentially support aluminium pricing through improved export inflows and reduced trade friction. | Jindal Aluminium Ltd plans to invest ₹100–150 crore to expand its Bhiwadi plant capacity by 60,000 tonnes per annum, after prior investments. The company cites rising demand and focuses on improving productivity and reducing costs through technology. The capacity boost increases near-term aluminium supply expectations.

news_summary: China’s June primary aluminium output rose to 3.46 million tonnes as smelters in Yunnan resumed production after power curbs were eased. With more hydropower availability and lower coal and alumina prices, daily output increased month-on-month. The supply boost from the top producer is likely to weigh on aluminium prices. | Copper prices fell on demand fears after weak Chinese economic data. Reuters reports softer stimulus expectations, rising non-ferrous output including aluminium, and signs inventories may grow, pressuring the copper spot premium and futures. Aluminium and nickel also declined on the same risk-off demand outlook.

news_summary: Copper prices rose Thursday as a weaker U.S. dollar made dollar-priced metals cheaper, alongside expectations the Fed’s rate-hike cycle is near its end. Markets also looked for Chinese property-sector stimulus, though China’s weak data tempered gains. The project includes an $8 million aluminium casting line in Taree, expected to complete by mid-2024, expanding capacity and reshoring slug supply. | Rio Tinto warned Australia’s carbon emissions scheme and insufficient renewable power make aluminium refining uneconomic, citing a $1.7bn write-off and $1.2bn loss linked to abatement costs. The tightening decarbonisation requirements and costly clean-tech investment threaten future capacity, creating bearish sentiment for aluminium supply and pricing. | Antilles Gold confirmed additional assay results at the El Pilar copper deposit in Cuba, including long intercepts with notable copper grades and shallow gold mineralisation. The company is progressing mine planning and prefeasibility for a joint venture open-pit “Nueva Sabana” operation, with ongoing metallurgy and negotiations for concentrate sales. | Analysts cut copper, aluminium, and nickel forecasts as supply ramps up. Copper faces a soft patch with mine output rising and a stated surplus widening. Aluminium prices likely face pressure as Yunnan smelters restart after hydropower-related curbs ease. Nickel drops amid stronger Indonesian production and higher-grade conversion. | Reliance Steel & Aluminum reported Q2 2023 results and expects Q3 tons down due to seasonal factors and one less shipping day. It also forecasts pricing decline and “temporary downward pressure” on gross margins, driven by declining prices for flat-rolled products including common alloy aluminum. | Copper edged higher on hopes that U.S. rate hikes have peaked, helped by dollar weakness. Aluminium and nickel also rose modestly despite demand worries.

Softer inflation supports a weaker dollar. news_summary: China urged the US to immediately lift Section 232 tariffs on Chinese steel and aluminum imports, arguing they violate WTO rules. The WTO panel found China’s countermeasures inconsistent and China says it will study the report. The dispute centers on tariff-driven trade flows, which can affect near-term aluminum demand expectations. | India’s ADC12 aluminium alloy ingot market was largely stable week-over-week, supported by range-bound scrap and corrections in US-linked futures. Primary producers cut prices by up to INR 3,750/t, while limited scrap supply and some regional premiums persisted. Demand is moderate and upcoming Maruti announcements are expected to limit near-term price declines. | SMM says China’s aluminium ingot stockpile fell to 490,000 mt as of Aug 17, down week-on-week and at a five-year low, driven mainly by fewer arrivals earlier in the week. The market is close to the prior five-year low and shortage of cast ingots may limit near-term supply. | China’s MOFCOM urges the US to lift Section 232 tariffs on Chinese steel and aluminum, citing a WTO panel ruling that the measures violate WTO rules. China argues the tariffs disrupt global trade and supports removing the 10% aluminum import levy to restore market access and trade flows. | Romanian aluminium producer Alro Slatina will ask shareholders to approve USD 150 million of syndicated loans. The financing is intended to roll over existing revolving credit facilities and support working capital. Because the news is mainly about refinancing and liquidity without stated production cuts, any effect on aluminium prices is likely minor in the near term. | Ball Corporation will sell its aerospace business for $5.6 billion to streamline operations and accelerate “low-carbon” aluminium packaging. Low inventories also supported prices. This supports aluminum sheet output. | China’s trade ministry urged the US to immediately remove Section 232 tariffs on Chinese steel and aluminium imports after a WTO tribunal ruled the US levies were illegal. The prospect of tariff relief would improve aluminium import prospects and likely support prices. | Copper prices rebounded after a third weekly fall, supported by sharp SHFE stock declines and low inventories plus expectations of autumn demand. news_summary: China’s August aluminium imports rose 20% month-on-month as demand hopes improved and domestic inventories stayed tight, with SHFE stocks falling below 100,000 tons. Imports increased further on higher Russian purchases. Aluminium production and bauxite imports also climbed, reinforcing active consumption and near-term support for prices. | Copper and other base metals were largely flat in Asia as markets awaited the U.S. Federal Reserve’s Sept. 20 rate decision and guidance. Analysts said the Fed outcome would steer metals and energy. Reported moves were minimal: copper near-flat, aluminium slightly higher on LME, and nickel marginally up. | Russia has increased alumina imports from India to feed its large Siberian aluminium plants after alumina supply was disrupted by a Ukraine suspension and Australia’s ban on exports to Russia. Rusal expanded sourcing (India/Kazakhstan) to keep output and contain costs, potentially supporting near-term aluminium supply. | Reuters reports copper sentiment was pressured by renewed China demand concerns tied to a worse property sector and by LME approved warehouse inventories rising more than 170% since mid-July. Despite support from expectations that global rate tightening is nearing its end, copper traded flat and pressured by piling stocks. | Reuters reports Glencore bought only small volumes of potential Rusal aluminium supply under an existing contract that may be extended to end-2025. After Glencore stopped new Russian purchases, existing obligations remain and unfulfilled backlog could release larger quantities to rivals, adding supply expectations and pressuring aluminium prices. | Crown Holdings and the International Aluminium Institute released a study outlining measures to strengthen beverage can recycling in several countries, aiming to reduce CO2 and increase recycling rates. Better collection and recycling systems should boost recycled aluminum supply versus primary production, potentially offering modest near-term support for aluminium prices.

Aluminium was flat, with no clear near-term catalyst. | Japan’s Q4 aluminium premium fell 24% to $97/t, the lowest since Jan–Mar, as producers’ sought levels were cut amid dull demand and large stockpiles at Japanese ports. Shipments of sheets/extrusions declined and Russian ingot imports dropped, reinforcing oversupply concerns and pressuring regional pricing. | Copper rallied 0.7% and nickel 1.3% on Monday after the U.S. passed a stopgap bill to avoid a government shutdown, boosting risk-on sentiment. news_summary: Copper prices eased and were set for their steepest weekly decline in nearly 11 months as a firm dollar and high LME inventories weighed on sentiment. Aluminium rebounded slightly but remained on track for its biggest weekly drop since February. Nickel also extended losses for a fourth straight session. | Reuters says China’s copper and aluminium demand has stayed robust despite housing stress, supported by appliances, EVs and renewables. Smelter inventories reportedly halved seasonally and procurement increased after a copper drop. Aluminium demand grew from solar and grid projects, lifting Chinese prices, though global macro demand risks persist. | Reuters reports that unexpectedly resilient Chinese demand for copper and aluminium—supported by appliances, EVs, solar/wind and some property stimulus—has steadied otherwise weak global copper prices and lifted Chinese aluminium prices. Copper smelter inventories reportedly fell to about half normal, and aluminium demand rose for renewables and grids. | Reuters reports copper rose Friday but is set for its biggest weekly drop in five months due to sustained high interest rates, a firm dollar, and elevated LME inventories. It links the fall to weaker-than-expected demand growth and CTA fund selling, while aluminium and nickel prices track the broader base-metal mood. | Metra S.p.A. completed the acquisition of Extruded Aluminum Corp. (Belding Machinery and Equipment Leasing LLC and subsidiaries) for undisclosed terms. EAC manufactures custom extruded aluminum profiles used in sectors including solar, transportation, and construction. The deal expands Metra’s North American extrusion and fabrication footprint; no capacity or policy changes are specified. | U.S. producers and unions filed a petition to impose antidumping and countervailing duties on aluminum extrusion imports from 15 countries, with potential margins above 100% for some origins. The scope is broad and could expand coverage beyond a prior 2011 China order, while existing Section 232/301 duties already apply. | US Aluminum Extruders Coalition filed an antidumping (ADD) petition and an additional countervailing duty (CVD) petition regarding aluminum extrusions from 15 countries, alleging dumping and subsidies. The filing seeks remedies for material injury/threat of injury to domestic producers, which could lift near-term US import costs and support aluminum prices. | A coalition of U.S. aluminum extruders and the United Steelworkers filed antidumping and countervailing duty petitions on aluminum extrusions from 15 countries. The petitions allege dumping and subsidies and could lead to U.S. duties, raising import costs, reducing supply availability, and increasing purchase prices in the near term. | U.S. industry groups filed new AD/CVD petitions covering aluminum extrusions from 15 countries. The scope may include certain downstream products previously excluded, with duties determined by the extrusion origin. Rates are cited from 28% to 256%, potentially imposed as early as late December, raising import costs. | BSE (via ICCL/RMS margin framework guidance) issued a notice outlining margin parameters for newly launched non-agri futures in aluminium and copper, with high volatility category requiring minimum initial margin of 10% and minimum MPOR of 3 days. It is a risk-management/trading framework update rather than a demand-supply shock. | BSE/ICCL issued a margin framework notice for newly launched non-agri futures on Aluminium and Copper (also Zinc), specifying high-volatility category initial margin of 10% and minimum MPOR of three days. The update affects trading/risk management for the contracts but not the metals’ underlying fundamentals.

Gains were modest due to a firmer U.S. dollar and higher Treasury yields as markets expected rates higher for longer. LME zinc, tin, lead fell while aluminium rose. Nickel was down on SHFE. | Romanian aluminium producer Alro announced a renewed multi-year contract with Airbus to supply aluminium flat rolled products used in aircraft manufacturing. The agreement renews the prior 2016 deal and highlights Alro’s capacity and ongoing investments. The new orders provide clearer demand visibility for aluminium. | Copper rose 0.6% on LME support from stronger-than-expected Chinese growth, consumption and industrial activity, plus hopes for additional stimulus. Gains were capped by a firmer dollar as U.S. yields approached 5%. Aluminium’s cash discount narrowed, indicating tighter LME inventories; nickel slipped slightly. | Ghana’s GIADEC aims to reduce reliance on imported aluminium products by expanding local downstream production. The piece cites Ghana’s large bauxite resources, a concession awarded to Rocksure, and existing use of VALCO output by local manufacturers for cables, hollowware and roofing sheets, supporting higher aluminium processing demand.

news_summary: Copper prices edged lower as the U.S. dollar strengthened following a surge in the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield. Three-month LME copper fell 0.1%. Aluminium was also slightly down, while nickel gained modestly, reflecting mixed but generally softer pricing linked to currency pressure and tempered China stimulus hopes. | Glencore plans to wind up Mount Isa copper operations in 2025, and politicians dispute the company’s claim the mine is not economically viable. The Queensland government is funding worker support and past smelter maintenance, while calls grow for state intervention to keep copper mining—and smelting—operating. | Oman’s Green Tech Mining & Services, backed by OMCO, plans to move from pilots to commercial production by reprocessing OMCO legacy sites. It targets copper output from 15 million tonnes of tailings and contaminated groundwater in Suhar starting from late 2023, with further ramp and gold production in 2024. Invested in a copper tailings remediation plant. | The piece argues aluminum demand is being supported by a growing aircraft backlog as air travel rebounds and production lags. Aerospace manufacturers use large shares of aluminum in aircraft components, and sanctions and supply-chain disruptions raise sourcing challenges, supporting firmer aluminum demand and potentially higher near-term prices. | Aluminum prices are described as sideways after an end-September spike and October pullback. The article links price behavior to U.S.-EU discussions on Section 232 aluminum exemptions and possible future tariff action, while noting Russian-origin discounts are being absorbed by buyers and overall price moves remain driven by broader trends.

news_summary: SMM’s aluminium morning comment reports SHFE 2312 aluminium slightly lower overnight, while LME aluminium edges up. It cites macro uncertainty (Fed hike expectations, Israel-Gaza conflict) and fundamentals: China operating capacity near a peak, mixed downstream orders, lower social-warehouse pick-ups, and no imminent inventory decline. Overall, prices expected rangebound short term. | HK-listed Rusal is reported to buy a 30% stake in Chinese alumina refinery operator Wenfeng. Because alumina refining underpins aluminium production, the move may strengthen supply confidence and marginally improve near-term expectations for aluminium availability and processing economics.

news_summary: Glencore said it expects full-year copper and zinc despite lower first-nine-month output, citing Cobar mine sale and weaker by-product production. It lowered full-year nickel guidance after sharp production drops and also adjusted ferrochrome targets. These updates are likely to affect copper and nickel market expectations near term; aluminium is not addressed.

news_summary: Copper prices edged down as the US dollar firmed, while traders awaited key US inflation data to gauge whether the Fed may raise rates further. Powell’s recent comments have tempered hopes the hiking cycle is ending. LME aluminium and nickel showed only slight moves, suggesting mainly macro-driven effects. | Goldman Sachs expects commodity prices to rise in 2024, citing a less hawkish Fed and lower recession risk. The note forecasts oil around $80–$100/bbl and is optimistic on industrial metals, projecting an average copper price of about $9,200/mt in 2024 and positive expectations for aluminium. | Goldman Sachs advises a long stance on commodities, citing a less hawkish Fed and lower recession risks that should support demand. It expects crude oil to trade within $80–100/bbl in 2024 and is also optimistic on copper and aluminium, projecting average copper around $9,200/mt in 2024. | Si6 Metals announced jagged high-grade copper-silver drill intersections at its Dibete project in Botswana, including 2m at 4.2% copper and 268g/t silver, tied to geophysical anomalies. The results strengthen the perceived copper sulphide potential in early drilling, supporting bullish sentiment for copper. | Canadian Copper reports trenching and exploration at its Murray Brook West project and updates on court-supervised efforts to restart the former Caribou Mine. The company notes existing toll-milling infrastructure and argues Murray Brook could extend mine life, but no clear near-term production or shipment figures are provided. | U.S. Commerce initiated antidumping and countervailing duty investigations on aluminum extrusions imported from 15 countries. Petitioners allege widespread dumping and subsidization and seek expanded scope, including previously excluded alloys and third-country processed products. Potential affirmative AD/CVD findings could raise import costs and tighten aluminum extrusion supply. | EU Raw Materials Week coverage highlights policy measures to secure strategic raw materials for net-zero technologies. It emphasizes copper’s role and the need to streamline permitting and lower energy costs for electricity-intensive production. It also notes recycling regulation could benefit metals including nickel, supporting medium-term supply expectations. | Environmental groups (Sierra Club and Environmental Integrity Project) intend to sue Alcoa’s Warrick aluminum smelter for Ohio River water pollution permit violations. They cite repeated excesses, including mercury and other discharge limits (including aluminum, copper, nickel). The notice allows 60 days to comply, implying potential compliance costs but no shutdown detail. | Aluminum prices are described as range-bound but facing bearish signals: global premiums have eased sharply, with U.S. Midwest and Europe duty premiums at multi-month lows. Norsk Hydro reported steep drops in aluminum sales, and LME dynamics show ample Russian material in warehouses amid weaker industrial and real-estate demand. | Ghana’s GIADEC and Rocksure International presented updated MRE results for bauxite at Nyinahin Block B, with resources revised upward (at least ~375MMT vs prior ~250MMT). The government-linked initiative aims for local refinery and smelter development, improving investor confidence for the Integrated Aluminium Industry and supporting aluminium sentiment.

news_summary: Copper prices eased from near six-week highs as a stronger U.S. dollar—supported by retail sales and cooling inflation data—reduced demand for dollar-priced commodities and reinforced expectations that the Fed will cut rates later. In the same report, LME aluminium and nickel also fell modestly. | GIADEC says mining of about half a million tonnes of bauxite from the Nyinahin range in Ghana will soon begin after completing a Mineral Resource Estimate and social impact analysis. The corporation highlights reserve size and responsible mining measures. The project could improve near-term expectations for aluminium feedstock supply. | Northstar Gold reported preliminary Phase I drilling results at the Cam Copper Mine, a historic VMS copper producer, with high-grade copper intersections (including very high Cu zones) and notes zones remain open for down-plunge/strike expansion. Assays are pending for some holes; no aluminium-linked impacts are mentioned. | Colossus Resources signed an option agreement to acquire 100% interests in Chilean Calvario and Mirador porphyry projects targeting copper (± Mo ± Au), with plans for at least $2.5m of exploration over two years. The news is exploration-stage and does not indicate immediate supply changes for tracked metals. | A caustic spill injured five workers at Alcoa’s Pinjarra alumina refinery in Western Australia. Emergency services responded on 14 November and injured staff were treated and released or expected to be released. The caustic is used to dissolve alumina from bauxite; the incident’s cause is under investigation. | A class action lawsuit was filed against Century Aluminum over dust and alleged harmful emissions of alumina/particulates from the Mount Holly smelter. The company acknowledged a malfunction, completed repairs (descaling and replacing collector bags), and said operations returned to normal. The dispute could raise short-term operating and compliance risk. | Ghana Integrated Aluminium Corporation (GIADEC) says feasibility work and a Mineral Resource Estimate support mining around half a million tonnes of bauxite from the Nyinahin hills in Ashanti Region, alongside environmental and social impact studies. Management also signals potential future refinery development, improving aluminium feedstock expectations. | Abitibi Metals announced a definitive agreement to earn up to 80% of the B26 polymetallic Cu–Zn-Au-Ag deposit in Quebec. The company highlights sizable historical copper resources and an exploration/PEA plan with drilling targeted in 2023–2024, potentially improving copper longer-term supply visibility, though near-term price impact is likely limited.

news_summary: London three-month copper rose to an eight-week high on a softer U.S. dollar and hopes that Chinese property support will improve demand. Reuters cites pledges for property financing and debt-risk resolution. LME aluminium also advanced and nickel increased, reflecting broader bullish sentiment across industrial metals. | SMM data on weekly aluminium ingot outflows from warehouses in China’s key consuming regions is presented as an indicator of physical market tightness. Higher ingot departures suggest lower readily available warehouse stocks for downstream buyers, which can reduce short-term supply and support nearby aluminium prices. | Copper prices rose to a two-month peak as Reuters linked gains to hopes of new China stimulus supporting property-related industrial demand, a softer dollar, and declining Shanghai Futures Exchange stocks. Supply concerns also emerged from protests disrupting ore processing at First Quantum’s Cobre Panama mine. Aluminium edged higher on the session. | Bonnell Aluminum said it is pleased with the ITC’s November 17, 2023 initial determination to open an investigation into allegedly unfairly traded imports of aluminum extrusions from 14 countries. The probe could lead to trade remedies that reduce import competition, supporting pricing expectations for aluminum extrusions. | Aleris won a multi-year deal to supply aluminum plates, sheets, and processed wing-skin material for Airbus aircraft manufacturing. Deliveries begin next year and build on Aleris’ existing Airbus qualification, including work for Airbus’ China facility. The announcement points to additional aluminum demand expectations.

news_summary: Copper prices climbed to two-month highs as supply risks intensified. An indefinite strike at Peru’s Las Bambas mine and maintenance at First Quantum’s Cobre Panama facility—both tied to protests blocking coal—supported LME and Shanghai copper gains. A weaker U.S. dollar further boosted the base-metals complex, lifting aluminium and nickel too.

news_summary: Copper prices pulled back from two-month highs as a firmer U.S. dollar and renewed growth concerns weighed on sentiment. However, declines were limited by a supportive supply-demand backdrop, including low inventories, supply disruptions, and strong Chinese demand. Aluminium and nickel also fell modestly amid the macro/dollar influence. | China’s primary aluminium net imports surged in Jan–Oct, with October imports up sharply and exports down, lifting net imports to over 1.06m mt. Russia led supply, though shipments slowed when the import window closed mid-October. Profitability is improving and the window appears to re-open, likely increasing inflows.

news_summary: People’s Daily profiles Guangxi Yinhai Aluminum’s “green aluminum” carbon-labeled electrolysis, powered by over half clean energy and improved efficiency. Clients reportedly purchase only labeled aluminium alloy plates. The firm also lowers electricity costs and extends recycling in the region, reinforcing near-term support for aluminium demand. | Emirates Global Aluminium (EGA) started building a pilot plant to convert caustic bauxite residue from its alumina refinery into manufactured soil (“Turba”). The project follows years of R&D and targets completion in 2024, aiming to reuse UAE bauxite waste locally. It is pilot-scale and mainly environmental/circular-economy focused. | Copper prices rose on the LME as a weaker U.S. dollar and a stronger yuan supported Chinese purchasing demand. The Yangshan premium hit a yearly high and front-running for potential China property stimulus boosted buying. LME nickel and aluminium were also slightly mixed in the session. | Saudi Arabia has already invested at least $12bn in aluminium industries and aims to double investment, targeting growth in mining and large projects. Officials say aluminium is prioritized for renewable energy, EV and aviation demand, and more projects are in the pipeline as part of an ongoing diversification strategy. | Great Southern Copper raised GBP905,000 via a placing to fund further exploration in Chile, including drilling at its Especularita copper-gold project and work at other targets, plus initial activity at its Monti Lithium project in Salar de Atacama. The funding is modest, with limited immediate implications for copper prices. | Copper rose on the LME as a firmer yuan and weaker U.S. dollar boosted Chinese buyers’ purchasing power for the dollar-priced metal. Front-running around potential China property stimulus further supported demand. Nickel was also higher and aluminium edged up, reflecting a broadly supportive base-metal tone.

news_summary: US aluminium stakeholders met with the Department of Energy to discuss decarbonizing and maintaining primary aluminium production. They argue IRA and infrastructure-law tools need greater electricity and renewables investment because high energy costs have idled or closed smelters. Calls for low-cost power could improve primary supply outlook, supporting aluminium prices. | Reuters reports copper edged higher as China’s central bank signaled accommodative policy and as a strike began at Peru’s Las Bambas copper mine, boosting supply-risk sentiment. Nickel rebounded from three-year lows after an industry report raised the prospect of changes to Indonesia’s nickel pricing. Aluminium slipped modestly. | Copper edged higher on hopes China’s central bank keeps policy accommodative and on a reported indefinite strike at Peru’s Las Bambas mine. Nickel rebounded from three-year lows after an industry report suggested changes to Indonesia’s nickel pricing. Aluminium slipped modestly as broader metal prices were mixed. | Benjamin Hill Mining announced two inaugural diamond drill holes at its Alotta copper-gold-molybdenum project in Yukon, confirming multiphase porphyry-style mineralization with copper and molybdenite indicators. news_summary: Copper prices rose after the dollar weakened and China’s Caixin factory activity unexpectedly expanded in November, supporting demand expectations for industrial metals. The article also shows aluminium up modestly and nickel roughly unchanged, suggesting a broader positive metals tone driven mainly by macro and China data. | Antam says it shifted bauxite sales to Indonesia’s domestic market to offset the impact of the June export ban. Bauxite production and sales rose 6% in Q3, after earlier declines. The policy also bans copper exports and aims to boost local downstream processing. | BSE issued a notice that Aluminum Futures Contracts in the commodity derivatives segment will be discontinued effective December 4, 2023, following an earlier introduction notice. Trading members are directed to refer to prior exchange documentation. The change mainly impacts aluminium derivatives trading/hedging rather than underlying metal fundamentals. | Copper rose to a three-month high as Caixin showed stronger-than-expected China factory expansion, while exchange inventories declined and tighter supply signals emerged (Panama issues, less concentrate availability). A softer U.S. dollar and expectations of earlier Fed easing provided additional tailwinds. Nickel and aluminium were also modestly higher. | WEF says low-carbon aluminium production costs could be up to 40% higher than conventional output, driven by electricity switching, storage, and newer smelting/refining technologies. While end-market price increases are estimated at 1–2%, investment needs are large and low-carbon share is still small, supporting tightening supply expectations as demand grows through offtakes.

news_summary: Norsk Hydro reportedly took a $571 million loss tied to a weak alumina market. Alumina weakness can indicate deteriorating refining economics and softer near-term aluminium production incentives, which typically weighs on expectations for aluminium prices in the coming days and weeks. | Reuters reports copper prices pulled back as bullish investors reduced positions ahead of the holidays after copper failed to hold above recent highs. Nickel was little changed, weighed by growing supply and weak consumption, while aluminium fell modestly. Overall, the moves reflect short-term positioning and demand/supply expectations. | Norsk Hydro said it will record a 5.09 billion Norwegian krone ($495 million) impairment in Q4 after “challenging alumina market conditions,” and its shares dipped on the news. The write-down relates to aluminum production operations, signaling weaker market outlook that can depress near-term aluminum pricing expectations.
