news_summary: Shanghai aluminium (Feb) and zinc rose to November highs as markets priced potential further Chinese rate cuts and expected Red Sea shipping delays. Traders cited delayed refined aluminium shipments via Suez. The same session saw copper at multiweek highs and nickel higher, reflecting broad metal risk-on and logistics supply friction. | Chinese alumina futures on SHFE surged to record highs after an explosion in Guinea, a major bauxite supplier. Traders cited fears of disrupted bauxite transport due to limited fuel and ongoing China winter cuts reducing alumina operating capacity. Stockpiles may limit the short-term squeeze, but sentiment remains bullish for aluminium-related costs. | Reuters reports aluminium prices in London rose to the highest since Nov 6 on Friday, driven by supply concerns after a fuel depot blast in Guinea (key bauxite supplier) raised fears of bauxite shortages for Chinese alumina smelters. Chinese alumina futures surged on fears of alumina feedstock shortages. The move was supported by a weaker dollar and technical/CTA buying, with Red Sea shipping delays adding risk.

news_summary: Alcoa announced it will shut its Kwinana alumina refinery in Western Australia, ending alumina production by Q3 and cutting jobs. The company cited age, operating costs, bauxite grades and market conditions. While the port continues to handle related flows, the closure may tighten near-term alumina availability impacting aluminium expectations. | China’s November 2023 bauxite imports rose MoM and slightly YoY, led by higher shipments from Guinea as the rainy season ended. Australia increased in November but not strongly over Jan-Nov. The Guinea oil depot explosion initially boosted then eased alumina futures concerns, with SMM saying bauxite supply was not materially hit short term. | A major Shandong aluminum smelter cut its January prebaked anode benchmark purchase price by 150 yuan/mt to 3,878 yuan/mt. SMM said export anode prices fell about $15–20/mt as raw material prices declined, with east China anode prices closing around 3,878–6,410 yuan/mt. | Pampa Metals finalized a follow-up diamond drill program for its Piuquenes copper-gold porphyry project in Argentina, targeting depth and lateral extension of known mineralization. The update is likely to affect near-term expectations for copper-related supply and investor sentiment, though it provides no nickel or aluminium-specific information. | Asian aluminium demand is expected to gradually recover, with Japan’s automotive sector supporting consumption and China approaching production capacity, encouraging more imports. Aluminium premiums in Shanghai and Japan showed recent strength versus earlier in 2023. A December fire in Guinea raised bauxite supply disruption concerns, adding upward cost risk.

news_summary: London copper held steady as a weaker U.S. dollar supported prices, while investors awaited China data for demand outlook. China’s unchanged policy rate and softer pre-holiday replenishment weighed, but mine-side disruption could reduce refined output. LME aluminium edged up, while nickel fell slightly amid mixed moves across metals. | Copper prices fell after the dollar strengthened and Chinese demand worries resurfaced when the central bank left key rates unchanged. Aluminium also declined and nickel retreated. Separately, Nyrstar will suspend zinc smelting in the Netherlands due to high energy costs, potentially tightening Europe’s zinc market but not directly tracked metals. | Reuters reports Russian aluminium in LME warehouses more than doubled in December, reaching 90% of warranted stocks. While blanket sanctions are absent, the LME tightened delivery rules around penal import duties and the UK barred post-Dec. 15 acquisitions for physical delivery. EU measures are also expanding, increasing uncertainty. | Rio Tinto said 2023 production of aluminum rose to about 3.3 million tons (up 9%), aided by full operations at the Kitimat smelter, and mined copper output increased to 620,000 tons (up 2%). The higher supply profile could pressure near-term copper and aluminum prices. | Rio Tinto said 2023 production of aluminum rose to about 3.3 million tonnes on a return to full Kitimat smelter output, while mined copper increased to about 620,000 tonnes. Refined copper fell due to a Kennecott smelter rebuild, with stable production expected in Q1 2024.

news_summary: Alcoa criticised “politicisation” of its decision to close the Kwinana alumina refinery near Perth. The company says it will stop alumina production later this year, cutting hundreds of jobs, citing market pressures and costs. The dispute challenges claims that government policies caused the shutdown. | Reuters reports copper fell as a firm U.S. dollar pressured dollar-priced commodities, but losses were tempered by hopes for additional China stimulus, including potential new debt issuance. The piece notes that macro expectations for rate cuts shifted, influencing broader metal sentiment, with aluminium slightly up and nickel modestly down. | India and the US have set up a Joint Monitoring Mechanism to support exports of specific steel and aluminum products to the US without additional duties. The US also agreed to allow larger volumes of aluminum and steel imports from India without supplementary tariffs, easing trade frictions and supporting aluminum sentiment.

news_summary: London copper edged lower as a firmer dollar pressured greenback-priced metals, with traders cautious ahead of China’s GDP and industrial-output releases. Nickel and aluminium had mild gains/declines in the same session. Market focus stays on near-term demand cues from top consumer China and FX rate expectations. | China’s primary aluminium output hit a record 41.59 million tonnes in 2023, though growth slowed to 3.7% due to power shortages and weather-related production cuts in the southwest (including drought in Yunnan). December output rose 4.9% year-on-year, alongside higher total nonferrous metals production. | London copper fell as China’s weaker growth data raised concerns about demand, while a firmer U.S. dollar weighed on dollar-priced commodities. LME copper dropped 0.5%. Nickel was slightly higher, but aluminium slid 0.5%, reflecting a broadly cautious macro tone for industrial metals. | China’s primary aluminium production rose 3.7% in 2023 to 41.59 million tonnes, with December output up 4.9% y/y. Total production of 10 non-ferrous metals increased 7.1%, including copper and nickel, suggesting broadly higher supply that could pressure industrial metal prices, especially aluminium. | K2 Gold reported rock/grab sampling results at its Mojave Project, including multiple samples at Soda Canyon with copper grades above 1% (notably 1.87% Cu) and trace-to-4.11% Cu across targets. The release focuses on exploration and EIS permitting timelines, with no production or delivery implications. | Aluminium prices eased in early January after a December rise, with markets largely brushing off 2023 alumina supply jitters. UK sanctions increased Russian-origin aluminium in LME warehouses (now ~90% of stocks), but LME/CME pricing correlation remains intact and premiums diverge, leaving net near-term direction uncertain.

news_summary: Copper prices rose in London as the dollar eased, rebounding from a six-week low, but remained on track for a fourth straight weekly decline. The report also notes LME aluminium up and nickel slightly down, reflecting mixed, currency-driven moves rather than a clear sustained trend. | China imported 3.06 million tonnes of unwrought aluminium in 2023 as consumption grew, supported by demand from solar and autos. Imports were encouraged by higher China prices and rising Russian shipments. December imports jumped, and bauxite imports also rose, underpinning aluminium feedstock availability. | Morgan Stanley suggests China’s copper and aluminium output is being absorbed rather than piling up, supported by resilient property completions and continued demand. It flags supply-side risks—aluminium cuts in Yunnan and copper disruptions in Chile/Panama—as upside factors. Citi also expects further copper strength on China easing expectations. | The piece expects solid near-term demand for copper and aluminium in China, with output at record highs but inventories indicating absorption rather than buildup. Aluminium edged up; nickel eased. | CopperCorp began a 2024 diamond drilling program at its Razorback rare earth and copper property in western Tasmania, testing Cu-Au and REE targets. The release cites historical high-grade intersections but does not indicate production, permitting disruptions, or supply changes. Thus, any near-term price impact is likely limited and sentiment-driven. | Alcoa reported Q4 EPS loss of $0.56 versus analysts’ $0.84 loss and revenue at $2.6 billion. news_summary: China’s imports of unwrought aluminium more than doubled in 2023, with Russian material’s share rising to 76%. Despite high LME warrant stocks of Russian aluminium, China’s demand is absorbing supply that might otherwise hit Western inventories. Sanctions are making Russian primary metal harder to sell to Western users. | London copper was subdued ahead of a U.S. Fed decision, with investors watching clues on a potential March rate cut. Prices are also supported by a slight monthly gain as Panama’s Cobre mine closure and lower global miner guidance disrupt supply and prompt possible Chinese smelter production cuts. | The report outlines recent aluminum sheet price trends across Asia, Europe, and North America, linking movements to fluctuating aluminum ingot prices, construction demand weakness, labor strikes, constrained supply, inventory levels, production costs, and improving automotive sentiment. It notes declines in Q1 and firmer prices in Q2 as inventories fall. | LME copper slipped 0.2% on softer China manufacturing data and traders’ caution ahead of the Fed decision. Although copper consumption rose in 2023, analysts expect slower growth. Sentiment also weighed on metals as smelters were urged to cut production amid tighter raw-material supplies, partially supporting prices. | International Aluminium Institute data show 2023 world alumina production slipped slightly, but H2 output rebounded, with December up month-on-month. Gulf alumina/feed supply expectations look supported by rising production and GCC capacity expansions and major investment, suggesting improved near-term aluminium supply outlook.

news_summary: China’s imports of unwrought aluminium more than doubled year-on-year in 2023, reaching about 1.54 million tonnes, the second-highest annual total this century. The increase suggests stronger Chinese demand for primary aluminium, with Russia cited as a key driver behind the import boom. | Human Rights Watch alleges major automakers’ China supply chains may include aluminum produced with forced labor in Xinjiang. With U.S. rules requiring proof against Xinjiang forced labor, buyers may avoid or re-source harder-to-trace Xinjiang material. That risk can tighten compliant aluminum supply and support aluminium prices. | Human Rights Watch alleges carmakers (Toyota, Volkswagen, Tesla, GM, BYD) may source aluminium linked to forced labour from Xinjiang smelters, where labour-transfer programs allegedly coerce Uyghurs. The report cites Xinjiang’s sizable aluminium output and warns of potential import restrictions and tougher sourcing scrutiny, which could disrupt demand. | An Australian court dismissed United Co. Rusal’s lawsuit seeking to restore its access to Rio Tinto’s Queensland Alumina refinery. The decision upholds Australian sanctions that restrict Rio Tinto/QAL from supplying bauxite and processing alumina for Rusal. As long as sanctions continue, Rusal’s alumina supply remains constrained. | China is expanding “green-power” aluminium using renewable electricity, with certification enabling buyers to source low-carbon metal. However, Yunnan smelters—key producers—face seasonal hydro shortages in the dry season, requiring rate cuts. This tightens near-term supply expectations and supports aluminium premiums versus ordinary metal. | Human Rights Watch says major automakers may be using aluminium linked to Uyghur forced-labour programs in China’s Xinjiang. It highlights US requirements for Xinjiang-related forced-labour proof since 2022 and notes tracing difficulty once aluminum is alloyed. The scrutiny may tighten access to certain aluminum supply chains. | Alcoa said it will supply Nexans with low-carbon aluminium rod made using ELYSIS, a process that eliminates direct greenhouse gas emissions from smelting by emitting oxygen instead. Nexans will start qualifications for rod in various cable types across Western Europe and Scandinavia, supporting sustainable electrification demand. | Copper prices fell on the LME after stronger dollar moves and receding expectations of a March U.S. rate cut. Losses were partly limited by tighter supply and improved China factory/export-order data, but demand is still cautious (cash-3m discount near highs). Aluminium and nickel also declined. | Alcoa signed a supply agreement with Nexans to provide low-carbon aluminum for several of Nexans’ European sites. The aluminum will come from Alcoa’s Elysis electrolysis process, cutting direct emissions. Alcoa already supplies Nexans with EcoLum aluminum. Contract value was not disclosed. | Evtec Aluminium, the planned acquisition target of Blackboxstocks, received a $67M long-term supply contract from JLR to produce parts for Jaguar’s new electric vehicle line. Evtec will be a single-source supplier for key EV powertrain components, adding new sales and supporting anticipated aluminium demand. | Alcoa said it will supply Nexans with low-carbon aluminum rods produced via ELYSIS technology, which eliminates direct GHG emissions from traditional smelting. The aluminum will be used in Nexans cable facilities across Western Europe and Scandinavia, spanning low to high-voltage cables. | Rio Tinto said Federal and NSW governments have begun assessing ways to secure stable, competitively priced power for the Tomago Aluminium smelter once its current electricity agreement ends in 2028. The decision follows ongoing talks about long-term energy planning for local manufacturing, supporting aluminium production cost outlook. | Human Rights Watch alleges automakers, including Tesla, GM, Volkswagen, Toyota and BYD, source aluminum tied to forced-labor practices in China’s Xinjiang region. With U.S. rules requiring proof goods are not made with forced labor, compliance uncertainty and potential scrutiny could disrupt aluminum flows and pressure prices.

news_summary: Human Rights Watch alleges major automakers, including Tesla, GM, Volkswagen and Toyota, may be using Chinese aluminium produced with Uyghur forced labor. The report highlights sourcing from China’s Xinjiang region and other areas, increasing the risk of compliance scrutiny and potential procurement curbs that could affect near-term aluminium flows and prices. | Indonesia’s bauxite export ban has led to a cash-flow squeeze as domestic alumina plants cannot absorb the surplus bauxite. Limited alumina capacity and bank reluctance to fund new smelters have caused some firms to cut output or pause production. Indonesia is trying to expedite additional alumina plants, but delays weigh on expectations. | Human Rights Watch links major automakers to aluminum allegedly produced with forced labor in China’s Xinjiang region. With governments requiring proof goods aren’t from forced labor and tracing difficult after refining/alloying, automakers may tighten sourcing away from Xinjiang supply. That could support aluminum prices near term. | Biden’s proclamations extend U.S. Section 232 tariff-rate quotas on EU steel and aluminum through Dec. 31, 2025, keeping quota volumes unchanged and reiterating eligibility rules for in-quota treatment (including primary aluminum certification). Some steel exclusion relief is extended; aluminum exclusions remain available. Copper is not addressed. | Ball Corporation said beverage and packaging customers will increasingly require low-carbon aluminium due to UN net-zero goals, SEC climate disclosures and the EU CBAM. It cites 85–90% recycled content and use of low-carbon primary aluminium via carbon-free smelting tech (e.g., Elysis, HalZero). The article notes Fastmarkets low-carbon aluminium differentials are trading higher in Europe. | Copper prices fell as traders weighed weaker China demand signals and patchy global industrial activity, alongside expectations of higher-for-longer U.S. rates. China and LME-tracked inventories rose sharply, cash copper discounts widened to a record, and stocks in warehouses limited the selloff. Aluminium dipped slightly; nickel rose.

news_summary: China’s exports of aluminium products to the EU that fall under the EU carbon border tariff (CBAM) fell 30% in 2023 to 689,000 tonnes. EU importers must report embedded emissions after CBAM’s launch in October, even though charges start in 2026, reducing Chinese shipment volumes. | Copper prices fell to their lowest in nearly two weeks as the dollar climbed and sentiment around China’s property sector worsened. Traders cited weak credit impulse and fading expectations of Fed cuts after strong U.S. jobs data. While mining issues offered some support, aluminium and nickel also edged lower. | AEROBAL says global aluminium aerosol can shipments rose 6% in 2023 to about 6.4 billion units, with demand stronger across EU, the Americas, and Africa, and a high order backlog into early 2024. The outlook is positive, while Germany’s primary aluminium output decline and production cost/regulatory concerns add supply tightness risk. | MetalX plans to build an aluminum recycling campus in Defiance, Ohio, featuring scrap shredding/sortation and an aluminum rolling-slab facility to convert scrap into slabs for sheet production. The project supports automotive, beverage, and building markets, with operations expected in the first half of 2027.

news_summary: London base metals rose Tuesday as the US dollar eased and risk sentiment improved after state-backed Chinese stock buying. Three-month LME copper gained 0.7%. LME nickel increased 0.7%, while LME aluminium edged up 0.1%. The article also cites pressure from weak SHFE demand ahead of Lunar New Year. | China’s metallurgical-grade alumina production rose year-on-year but fell modestly month-on-month, with several provinces cutting operating rates due to ore shortages and pollution warnings that restricted calcination capacity. Some plants resumed in early February. Overall, tighter alumina supply conditions are supportive for aluminium prices. | SMM reports Yunnan’s electrolytic aluminum operating capacity is about 4.49 million tons, unchanged. A dry season keeps hydropower contribution low, reducing expectations for higher industrial electricity demand. Enterprises have no immediate plans to restart production and await clearer power-sector policy. | Fastmarkets updated its methodology for the aluminum P1020A all-in price delivered to the US Midwest. The formula previously used LME unofficial cash PM bid prices, which were discontinued after a 90-day notice. The new benchmark uses LME cash AM bid plus a Fastmarkets Midwest premium, published daily. | EV Minerals announced a binding LOI to acquire 100% of the Baldwin and Lunge nickel exploration properties near Sudbury, Ontario, with immediate sampling planned. The projects’ geology indicates potential nickel and copper sulphide mineralization. However, there is no near-term production change, so price effects are likely limited to sentiment. | Human Rights Watch alleges major automakers source aluminum that may involve Uyghur forced labor in Xinjiang, noting traceability difficulties as Xinjiang aluminum is mixed into broader supply chains. The report raises prospects of EU/US forced-labor import restrictions, but the article does not show immediate aluminum supply disruption.

news_summary: Shanghai nickel rallied to a three-month high as investors priced potential U.S. sanctions on Russian metals, raising supply-risk expectations. The Shanghai March contract rose 5.1% and LME nickel held around multi-week highs. Russia’s role as a key refined nickel and aluminium producer supported broader gains. | IGO reported higher lithium volumes but lower nickel output in Australia, citing weaker nickel grades and recoveries at Nova and reduced concentrate production at Forrestania following mine closure. It also placed the Cosmos nickel project into care and maintenance due to the global nickel price slump, with LME nickel down sharply year-on-year. | ABB won an order for four electromagnetic stirrers to support expansion of a U.S. aluminum melting and casting complex. The technology is claimed to boost productivity, lower energy use, and reduce dross, improving furnace efficiency and yield. This strengthens near-term expectations for aluminum production and supply. | SMM’s afternoon review shows broad gains in domestic base metals: Shanghai nickel rose ~4.1% and aluminum edged up, while spot copper prices in Guangdong (1# electrolytic and wet) increased day-on-day. The piece cites higher Shanghai premiums and constrained shipments, despite weak downstream pickup and concerns about next week’s warehouse receipts. | SMM’s afternoon review shows domestic base metals mostly rising: Shanghai nickel jumped 4.1% and alumina and aluminium were higher, while Shanghai copper and copper spot premiums also increased. Spot copper in Guangdong rose versus the previous day amid improved arrivals but cautious downstream buying. Oil eased slightly; broader macro was mixed. | Nickel prices were set for the biggest weekly gain in seven months as new Western sanctions against Russia spurred covering of bets on lower nickel prices. LME three-month nickel rose to about $17,510/t. The piece notes aluminium’s reaction was muted and copper had a milder supportive bounce.

news_summary: Tomago Aluminium plans to issue a large wind and solar energy tender to transition its electricity-intensive NSW smelter to renewable power, following the expiration of an AGL contract in 2028. The firming deal would ensure stable, economically sustainable power supply for aluminium production, potentially supporting near-term supply expectations. | Tomago Aluminium will issue a major tender seeking long-term wind and solar power supply to run its NSW smelter on greener electricity, with the current AGL contract expiring in 2028. As an energy-intensive producer, the firmed renewable deal can improve production cost certainty for aluminium. | Tomago Aluminium plans to tender for large wind and solar supply to transition its energy-intensive NSW smelter to clean power. The site needs about 950MW to operate and currently relies on an AGL contract expiring in 2028, with the new supply targeted from 2029. It follows related Rio Tinto renewables deals. | Rusal reported an 84.3% annual profit slump to $282 million, citing weak realised aluminium prices and reduced production amid international sanctions. The Reuters report highlights how sanctions are affecting the aluminium producer’s output, while weaker pricing weighs on earnings, potentially influencing short-term aluminium price sentiment. | Rusal’s 2023 profit fell sharply as realised aluminium prices weakened and output faced pressures from sanctions. Average realised aluminium prices dropped 18% and alumina production fell 13.8% due to curtailment at Nikolaev refinery after martial law. Western consumers reportedly shun new Russian metal deals, despite no direct Western sanctions. | Shanghai copper jumped to a near three-year high and LME copper to a 10-month peak as concerns grew that China—top refined copper producer—will lower smelter operating rates, adjust maintenance, and postpone new projects. Aluminium and nickel were also higher, helped by supportive supply factors. | Chance Aluminum Corporation is seeking a Letter of Intent to purchase county-owned land in Brady Township for a larger aluminum foil manufacturing facility serving the EV battery market. The project is still in early due diligence with no timeline, but it signals potential incremental U.S. aluminum foil demand growth.

news_summary: China’s primary aluminium output rose 7.4% in March, reaching 3.59 million tonnes, as rising aluminium prices and stronger demand improved industry profits. Contracts on SHFE and LME increased in March, with supply uncertainty in Yunnan adding support. Copper and nickel output also hit records. | The overnight report links higher base metal prices to new US/UK sanctions on Russian imports of aluminium, copper and nickel. It describes a risk-off tape for equities and safe havens, but notes base metals rose in response to the sanctions, supporting related miners’ share performance. | Osisko Metals reported preliminary metallurgical and grindability testwork for its Gaspé Copper Project, showing ~92% average copper recoveries, ~65% molybdenum recoveries, and commercially encouraging concentrate grades and flotation performance. The company says results feed into an ongoing PEA and updated resource work, without any near-term production commitment. | The US and UK announced sanctions blocking Russian aluminium from LME/CME delivery and banning imports, with similar restrictions for Russian copper and nickel. Aluminium prices jumped immediately, as non-Russian deliverable supply was expected to tighten. RUSAL said output continues, but deliverable warrants are dominated by Russia. | Washington and London prohibited metal exchanges from accepting new Russian-produced aluminium, copper, and nickel and barred imports into the U.S. and Britain. The change removes delivery of Russian Class 1 nickel from Global Commodities Exchange, following recent sanctions, tightening supply and supporting higher prices for these metals. | Australia’s federal government plans to provide $400m in loans to a Queensland alumina facility and fast-track support for a graphite project. news_summary: Biden said he will urge the US Trade Representative to consider tripling tariffs on steel and aluminium imports from China, contingent on an investigation finding anti-competitive practices. If implemented, the higher tariffs would raise China-origin aluminium costs and likely support US aluminium pricing in the near term. | The US President Biden dismissed fears of a renewed trade war while tripling tariffs on Chinese steel and aluminium imports. The policy aims to bolster support in US battleground states and is likely to affect aluminium supply and pricing near term by raising costs of Chinese imports. | In a Pittsburgh speech, President Biden urged increased tariffs on foreign steel and aluminum. Tariffs can reduce aluminum imports and improve the competitiveness of domestic supply, which may tighten availability and lift expectations for near-term aluminum prices. Direct impact magnitude depends on implementation timing and scope. | The article discusses a Biden proposal to triple U.S. tariffs on Chinese steel and aluminum. It notes that earlier tariffs increased steel prices and shifted import flows. The same tariff pressure on Chinese aluminum could raise U.S. aluminium import costs, supporting upward near-term price expectations. | BHP says fresh US/UK sanctions affecting Russian metal flows and an LME trading ban on certain Russian metals will squeeze existing supply. The piece also cites expected refined copper supply pressure (from concentrate constraints) over 3–6 months and notes BHP’s nickel outlook could change in Western Australia. | Biden said he will urge the US Trade Representative to consider tripling tariffs on aluminium imports from China if an anti-competitive practices investigation confirms subsidised, dumping behaviour. He accused China of funding steel producers and routing imports via Mexico, and described related actions to protect US industrial jobs. | China’s bauxite imports rose 4% YoY in Jan-Feb 2024, but domestic supply remains tight as some mines stopped before CNY. Refineries increased imported ore use, lifting prices; Guinea CIF bauxite rose to $71/mt vs $58/mt from Australia. SMM expects shipments to stay active and supply improve modestly. | Aluminium rod production rose in March on new capacity and capacity recoveries, but shipments stayed weak due to downstream caution from high aluminium prices. Some regions reduced output amid competition and inventory. With surplus supply and persistent inventory pressure into April, processing fees look stable, suggesting bearish near-term aluminium sentiment. | Biden urged the USTR to consider tripling existing Section 301 tariffs on Chinese steel and aluminum, citing unfair competition and subsidized, emissions-intensive Chinese production. The proposed higher tariffs would likely reduce Chinese import flows into the U.S., tightening supply conditions—most directly supportive for aluminium prices over coming days to weeks. | China’s Commerce Ministry said it opposes new U.S. restrictive measures on Chinese steel and aluminum products, which could triple tariffs. China called the steps unilateral and urged the U.S. to cancel additional tariffs, warning they could undermine global industrial supply chains and prompting retaliation risk. | Brazil’s aluminium industry is accelerating decarbonization via renewable power, switching refineries away from coal/heavy fuel oil, and expanding scrap treatment to raise recycled content. Producers also prepare for EU CBAM to avoid imports of higher-emissions material. The article frames a premium for greener aluminium and improved recycling economics.

news_summary: Nickel prices rose to seven-month highs as market talk of China planning state stockpile purchases triggered concerns about tight supply. Anticipated buying of nickel pig iron, plus wider bullish sentiment and a Russia metals ban raising disruption fears, supported gains not only in nickel but also aluminium and copper. | South32 said Q3 alumina production edged up to 1.240 million tonnes from 1.239 million tonnes a year earlier, while quarterly aluminium output fell slightly to 278,000 tonnes from 279,000. FY2024 production and cost guidance is unchanged; cyclone Megan impacts only its Australia Manganese operations. | Copper’s uptrend accelerated as UK and US sanctions target Russian exports of key metals, including copper, aluminium and nickel, to major exchanges (LME/CME). The supply curbs coincide with mining disruptions and improving Chinese industrial activity. Funds also boosted net longs, raising the risk of a speculative push. | South32 said Q3 copper production fell 10% y/y to 13,900 tonnes and was down 11% from the prior quarter, though it kept FY guidance unchanged. Aluminium output was slightly lower q/q, and nine-month nickel production declined. Overall results are mixed but copper deterioration is the clearest near-term negative signal. | China’s customs data shows March bauxite imports of 11.86 million mt, up 5.19% from February but slightly down year-on-year. Guinea remained the largest supplier. Australia’s imports surged MoM, boosting overall feedstock availability for alumina production, which may pressure aluminium prices near term. | Nornickel cut Q1 nickel production 10% y/y to 42,000 tonnes and said sanctions have constrained development. The U.S. and U.K. also barred imports of Russian nickel (and other metals), limiting export revenues. Together, the supply reduction and trade restrictions point to bearish near-term nickel pricing. | Nigeria’s ALSCON aluminium smelter near Ikot Abasi remains idle, missing another restart deadline after the planned 2023 timeline. The plant, owned 85% by UC Rusal and 15% by Nigeria’s government, has been delayed since 2007 due to financing and contract issues. The latest setback weakens prospects for local aluminium output. | Power Nickel reported thick, high-grade drill assay results from its Lion Discovery, including significant copper and nickel grades alongside PGMs, gold and silver. The update improves market sentiment about potential future Cu/Ni supply, but as an exploration-stage event it is unlikely to materially move prices within days to weeks; aluminium has no direct impact.

news_summary: U.S. Commerce issued affirmative preliminary antidumping determinations on aluminum extrusions from 14 countries and concurrent countervailing investigations for selected origins. CBP began collecting antidumping cash deposits from May 7, adding to countervailing duties. The scope was clarified and comments invited ahead of July final determinations, pending possible ITC injury findings.

news_summary: Wisconsin Aluminum Foundry (WAF) plans to expand after acquiring Iowa casting maker ATEK Metal Technologies. WAF produces aluminum and copper-based alloy castings and is also investing $25 million to add casting and 3D sand printing capacity. The move may slightly lift near-term aluminum demand expectations, but scale is unclear. | Copper in London edged higher on improved China trade data and property-support measures that may bolster near-term demand, with gains capped by a firmer US dollar. SHFE showed copper slightly down, nickel lower, and aluminium higher. | Greenwave Technology Solutions reported it expects 2024 revenues above $40 million, driven by increased capacity at its Cleveland and Virginia Beach metal recycling facilities. Removing the tariff should change near-term aluminum supply flows into the Mexican market, influencing expectations for tighter availability and therefore affecting aluminum prices over the coming days to weeks.

Systematic buying reportedly amplified the move, though physical import premiums stayed weak. Aluminium also rose modestly alongside the metals rally. | Copper surged to record highs on Monday after China announced “historic” property support steps and industrial output beat expectations. Momentum/investment buying and a Comex short-squeeze lifted global prices, despite weak physical import premiums into China. LME and SHFE aluminium and nickel also rose sharply, reflecting broad base-metals strength. | Copper surged to record levels on the LME and COMEX, up on short position covering and speculative fund buying, with some Chinese players contributing. Analysts still cite China fundamentals and high ShFE inventories as concerns. Aluminium also rose modestly and nickel gained, suggesting supportive momentum across industrial metals. | The Biden administration plans to increase Section 301 tariffs on $18 billion of China-origin imports, including aluminum. Tariffs on certain aluminum products would rise from 0–7.5% to 25% in 2024, with additional product-level details to follow in the Federal Register. Effects may support domestic aluminum pricing. | Alpha HPA has made a final investment decision to proceed with Stage Two of its HPA First project at Gladstone/Yarwun, backed by positive DFS outcomes and A$520m financing. Construction is slated to start mid-2024, targeting up to 10,000 tonnes/year of high-purity aluminium materials and related alumina outputs. | Rio Tinto declared force majeure on alumina shipments from its Queensland refineries because of gas shortages. The supply disruption of alumina, a key feedstock for aluminium refining, could limit downstream production and inventories, likely increasing near-term scarcity concerns and pressuring aluminium prices lower. | Rio Tinto has declared force majeure on alumina cargoes from its Queensland refineries after shortages of gas used to power operations. The firm notified customers not to expect scheduled shipments, indicating near-term alumina supply disruption that could constrain aluminium production and lift aluminium prices. | Rio Tinto has declared force majeure on Australian alumina cargoes. The disruption can limit alumina supply to refineries, tightening inputs for aluminium production. That near-term supply risk typically supports aluminium prices over the next few trading sessions as the market reprices delivery disruptions. | The U.S. International Trade Commission is investigating ArcelorMittal’s complaint that VinFast violated patents related to high-strength aluminum and aluminum alloy-coated steel in imported vehicles. ArcelorMittal claims VinFast lacks a license for VF 8 and other models. The piece provides no immediate ruling or ban.

news_summary: A Federal Register notice from BIS ends 12 general approved exclusions from U.S. Section 232 tariffs on steel and aluminum. The removed exclusions include multiple aluminum categories (e.g., tubes, alloy sheets, foil, bars, rods). The changes take effect July 1, 2024, potentially dampening aluminum demand. | Aluminum prices rose to a 23-month high as ongoing production issues in Australia reduce supply availability. The tighter market conditions for primary aluminum are expected to increase near-term buying interest and support spot prices, reflecting immediate supply disruption risk rather than a long-term demand change. | Copper prices trended lower as a steady U.S. dollar weighed on demand and China’s physical market weakened after record highs. Higher SHFE warehouse inventories reinforced bearish sentiment. LME aluminium and nickel also edged down, reflecting broader risk-off moves in industrial metals as markets awaited Fed minutes. | Chinese researchers developed a stronger, heat-resistant aluminum alloy using nanoparticles to maintain mechanical strength at high temperatures for aerospace engine components. The university says it is over six times stronger than traditional alloys and expects industrial use soon, which may modestly support future aluminum demand, but near-term price impact appears limited. | MX Holdings and Century Aluminum are considering Columbiana County for a $130 million high-tech secondary aluminum billet casting facility. The venture plans to use recycled scrap to produce low-carbon billets and aims for production in Q4 2026. Approval includes intent to reimburse expenditures via tax-exempt bonds. | Resources Minister Madeleine King warned that BHP’s underinvestment in ageing infrastructure could force thousands of workers into hardship, with BHP flagging it may close its Western Australian nickel operations. She linked the situation to Alcoa’s recent alumina refinery shutdown at Kwinana, citing similar consequences for supply. | Vedanta Aluminium, India’s largest aluminium producer, will not add new coal-fired power and is securing 1.3 GW of solar/wind renewables to raise renewable power use. The firm also plans capacity expansion to 3 million tonnes by 2026 and expects a bauxite mine environmental clearance, supporting output growth. | Prospect Ridge Resources announced new high-grade copper-bearing quartz vein showings on its Holy Grail property near the Copper Ridge zone, including samples up to ~14.8% Cu. The company plans to initiate drill holes on Copper Ridge in summer 2024. The release is exploration-focused and does not affect near-term supply materially. | The news notes Glencore will keep its Russian aluminum contract, but physical metal flows are becoming scarce. That suggests delivery disruptions and reduced near-term availability, which can tighten the aluminium market and support prices over the following days to weeks. | ElvalHalcor reported Q1’24 trading results, noting that both aluminium and copper metal prices averaged lower than in Q1’23. The company said revenue and segment profitability were pressured by the metal-price decline and subdued European demand. No new operational or regulatory catalyst for copper or aluminium prices was announced. | ElvalHalcor’s Q1’24 trading update links weaker financial results to lower LME benchmark metal prices and subdued industrial demand. It reports average aluminium and copper prices below the prior-year quarter, with reduced segment revenues and profitability. The company expects ongoing geopolitical and macro headwinds through 2024. | US policy and industry demand are pushing aluminium producers to decarbonize and standardize “low-carbon” definitions. DOE selected Century Aluminum for negotiations on $500m funding for a green smelter to potentially double capacity, while Fastmarkets launched low-carbon differentials. Curtailments from high power costs underscore the market impact. | Glencore and Rusal extended their long-term aluminium supply contract into 2025 despite previously low traded volumes. The extension is enabled because Rusal is not directly targeted by Western sanctions. The report also notes an LME ban on Russian aluminium, copper and nickel from April 13, reinforcing near-term supply-tight sentiment. | Cameroon exported 3,011 tons of aluminum sheets in 2023, up about 90.8% year-on-year, with revenue rising 80.2% to CFA6.6bn. The gains are expected to strengthen Alucam, whose exports and profitability have been under pressure since Rio Tinto exited in 2014. | US producers filed antidumping and countervailing duty petitions against Chinese imports of disposable aluminum containers, pans, and trays, alleging high dumping margins and potential subsidies. The case proceeds via ITC preliminary injury review (June 28, 2024) followed by Commerce preliminary AD/CVD decisions, which could raise import costs.

news_summary: Copper prices fell after hawkish Fed minutes supported a firmer U.S. dollar, making dollar-priced commodities more expensive. The drop was reinforced by weakening Chinese demand as earlier price gains reduced end-user consumption and lifted inventories. LME and SHFE aluminium and nickel also declined, reflecting broad risk-off pressure. | Washington will provide nearly $5 million to Kaiser Aluminum’s Spokane-area plant to replace two aging steam boilers under the state’s Climate Commitment Act. The funding offsets emissions-reduction costs and requires Kaiser to match the amount. The retrofit is expected to cut the plant’s annual greenhouse gases by about 13,000 metric tons. | Copper on the LME fell for a third straight session due to profit-taking and consumers shunning near-record prices. It notes elevated China inventories and a refined market surplus in March. The piece also shows aluminium and nickel easing on the session, alongside caution ahead of U.S. jobs data. | The USTR released a Federal Register notice outlining higher Section 301 tariffs on $18 billion of China-origin imports, with increases effective Aug. 1, 2024 (and later dates for 2025/2026). The targeted product list includes aluminum, and the notice also describes exclusion and comment processes.

news_summary: Copper prices were nearly unchanged as investors reassessed the rally that recently pushed LME prices to record highs, supported by fund buying and a U.S. Comex short squeeze but offset by dampened Chinese physical demand. LME aluminium and nickel fell slightly, indicating limited immediate directional impact beyond copper’s cooling momentum. | Hindalco Industries reported Q4 profit above analyst expectations, saying lower costs outweighed weak aluminium prices. The better earnings from the major India producer of both aluminium and copper supports margin resilience sentiment. The news may help underpin near-term expectations for aluminium pricing and, to a lesser extent, copper demand and production outlook. | Hindalco reported Q4 profit above expectations, saying lower costs helped offset weak aluminium prices. The result suggests current aluminium pricing remains soft, but the article is primarily about company earnings rather than a direct market shock. Copper is mentioned only as part of the producer mix, with no specific copper impact. | SMM says China’s alumina spot prices jumped on tight bauxite availability and rising refinery utilisation constraints. Demand rose as Yunnan restart of aluminium smelting increased alumina needs. Globally, outages at Rio Tinto/NALCO/Alcoa tightened supplies, pushing Western Australia FOB alumina higher and curtailing China imports. | Hindalco said India’s copper and aluminium demand should remain robust in FY25, citing rising electrification-led demand. It reported a 15% YoY increase in domestic refined copper demand in the latest quarter and higher copper and aluminium revenues/EBITDA, alongside favourable input costs, boosting sentiment toward both metals. | Hindalco’s management said Indian demand for refined copper rose 15% year-on-year to 225 KT in the latest quarter, helped by household electrification and other industrial uses. It also reported improved aluminium upstream revenue and EBITDA, expecting robust copper and aluminium demand in FY25. | Cameroon’s aluminum export revenues rose 11% to CFA54.2 billion in 2023, but export tonnage stayed almost unchanged at about 43.9k tons. The improvement is attributed to higher revenues for Alucam, despite ongoing financial and cash-flow challenges after Rio Tinto exited and the firm became state-owned.

news_summary: Shanghai aluminium jumped about 3% to a two-year high, with LME aluminium also near multi-year peaks. The report attributes the rally to strong China demand for aluminium used in solar and EVs, alumina supply disruptions, and higher premiums for primary metal shipments, supporting immediate bullish price pressure. | Aluminium is reported to be at a two-year high as China enforces or tightens capacity limits. The headline suggests these restrictions reduce nearby supply, lifting market expectations and supporting aluminium prices in the near term. | Shanghai aluminium hit a two-year high on early trade, supported by supply constraints linked to western sanctions on Russian metals, tight availability (including higher premiums to Japan), and robust demand from solar and EV sectors. Meanwhile, copper and nickel slipped as a firmer U.S. dollar weighed on base metals priced in dollars. | Norsk Hydro says it has started delivering power from onsite solar panels and battery storage at its aluminium plants in Sweden, with rooftop and ground-mounted PV plus 4.5 MW of batteries. Hydro expects lower energy costs and reduced carbon footprint as it transitions toward zero-emission extrusion. | Copper fell 2.7% on the LME as funds unwound bullish positions amid concerns that high interest rates will damp metals demand, especially from China. A firmer dollar and higher yields added pressure. Aluminium and nickel also declined, reflecting broader industrial-metals risk-off sentiment. | A trade association warns the UK construction sector may inadvertently use Russian aluminium because the EU has not sanctioned aluminium slabs, ingots and billets, comprising most Russian aluminium imports to the bloc. The UK and London Metal Exchange previously limited Russian aluminium, but EU gaps could keep supply flowing. | Norsk Hydro plans an $85 million HyForge technology investment at its Henderson, Kentucky aluminium recycling plant, expanding capacity by 28,000 tonnes and adding 31 jobs. The move, supported by Kentucky tax incentives, targets low-carbon aluminium billets for U.S. automotive production, following related upgrades at the site. | Australia’s Industry Minister Ed Husic is opening public consultation on a green metals policy focused on decarbonised production, including alumina and aluminium. The discussion paper cites Australia’s renewable and mineral advantages and highlights the firm renewable power needed for aluminium. The move may lift expectations for aluminium industry investment. | Aluminum prices reportedly reached a two-year high as Chinese smelters operated under output limits. The restriction on production tightens available supply in the near term, typically lifting spot prices. The move suggests market expectations for aluminium availability have worsened over the next days to weeks. | New Zealand Aluminium Smelters secured 20-year electricity arrangements totaling 572 MW to power the Tiwai Point aluminium smelter, with possible demand-response curtailments of up to 185 MW. Contracts start July 2024, supporting stable, competitive low-carbon aluminium output. Rio Tinto also moves to full ownership of NZAS. | Meridian, Mercury, Contact Energy and New Zealand Aluminium Smelters have signed a new electricity agreement, securing operation of the Tiwai Point aluminium smelter for 20 years after 18 months of talks. Local officials described the decision as major economic support, implying more stable aluminium output. | New Zealand’s Tiwai Point aluminium smelter (NZAS/Rio Tinto) secured a fixed-price power contract with Meridian Energy through 2044, ending uncertainty about shutdown after this year. The deal supports ongoing low-carbon aluminium output and includes demand-response provisions, which can improve power system reliability. | Rio says it will keep its New Zealand aluminum smelter running after power deals secured electricity on workable terms. The update lowers the near-term risk of shutdowns or reduced output tied to power costs, supporting aluminum supply expectations and likely providing modest upward price pressure for the metal. | Rio Tinto said it will keep its New Zealand aluminium plant operating for 20 more years. The extension improves long-term supply certainty for aluminium, but because the horizon is far beyond the next few weeks, near-term price impact is likely limited. | New Zealand’s Tiwai Point aluminium smelter is secured for the next 20 years after Meridian, Mercury, Contact Energy and New Zealand Aluminium Smelters signed a deal following 18 months of negotiations. Local officials say the smelter’s continued operation is a major economic boost for Southland and the country. | New Zealand Aluminium Smelters signed 20-year electricity arrangements totaling 572 MW to power the Tiwai Point aluminium smelter, using renewable generation in the mix. Rio Tinto says the long-term future of the smelter is secured, and it will buy remaining minority stakes in NZAS after the deal. | Rio Tinto agreed to buy Sumitomo Chemical’s stake to become sole owner of New Zealand Aluminium Smelters, which runs the Tiwai aluminium smelter. The company also secured 20-year electricity supply and demand-response arrangements with NZ energy generators. Rio also plans to buy Sumitomo’s stake in Australia’s Boyne Smelters.

news_summary: Sumitomo Chemical said it will sell its shares in New Zealand Aluminium Smelters and Boyne Smelters to Rio Tinto, after required approvals. The company cited profitability volatility tied to international aluminum market conditions and is exiting the investment to strengthen its financial position, not to change smelter output immediately. | A broker wrap flags tightening bauxite and alumina supply, citing disrupted Chinese domestic sources and ongoing refinery or mine constraints. With China importing most bauxite (mainly Guinea and Australia), the expected tighter alumina/bauxite balance should lift prices for longer, benefiting aluminium-linked producers and supporting aluminium sentiment. | Weaker US economic data revised GDP down and reinforced “higher-for-longer” rate concerns, dragging US risk assets. The report says base metals traded lower. It also mentions Rio Tinto keeping a New Zealand aluminium smelter running with new electricity supply agreements, but overall the tone is bearish for industrial metals. | Copper rebounded on the LME, but gains were capped by profit-taking after May 20 record highs and by weaker physical demand in China. China’s May manufacturing PMI fell below 50. Aluminium and nickel also rose on the day, reflecting broader market moves rather than a single new metal-specific shock. | Reuters reports copper under pressure after China’s May manufacturing PMI fell below 50, worsening demand expectations for the top consumer. Despite earlier speculative gains and a modest monthly rise, profit-taking could push copper back below $10,000. LME aluminium eased, nickel edged up slightly. | City of Bakersfield reports an uptick in copper wire theft from streetlights, leaving areas dark and creating repair costs far exceeding scrap value. Officials cite small stolen weights (often under $50) but high materials and labor bills. The city is considering more security and using aluminum alternatives for lower recycle value. | Rio Tinto will take full control of New Zealand Aluminium Smelters (NZAS) by buying Sumitomo Chemical’s stake. The move keeps the Tiwai Point aluminium smelter running for at least two more decades, supported by long-term electricity supply deals. Rio also buys more in Australia’s Boyne smelter. | Rio Tinto agreed to acquire Sumitomo Chemical’s stakes in New Zealand Aluminium Smelters (NZAS) and Boyne Smelters, moving toward full control of NZAS and a larger share of BSL. NZAS also signed 20-year renewable electricity contracts, stabilizing power costs and supporting ongoing aluminium smelting operations. | India’s mines ministry said aluminium production rose 1% in April to 3.42 lakh tonnes versus 3.39 lakh tonnes a year earlier. Iron ore and limestone also increased, indicating firmer activity in metals-linked user sectors, but aluminium output is the only directly cited tracked-metal driver.

news_summary: London copper rose 1.1% on supportive China and US data: China’s private manufacturing survey showed the fastest activity growth in about two years, and US inflation stabilization kept the Fed’s planned rate cuts on track. A tighter China copper import discount suggested improved physical demand, lifting copper and supporting aluminium and nickel. | Copper prices rose after stronger-than-expected Chinese factory activity and U.S. inflation data that revived hopes for Federal Reserve rate cuts. Lower expected rates could lift manufacturing and industrial-metal demand while a weaker dollar makes dollar-priced metals cheaper. Aluminium also moved higher in the same session. | The Aluminum Association says North American primary aluminium needs about $60 billion investment to cut emissions and reach net zero by 2050, alongside major reductions milestones. It cites low-carbon smelting, expanding recycling, and policy support for a green smelter project under the BIL/IRA, but targets are long-term. | Hydro Aluminum Metals USA plans to expand its Henderson, Kentucky facility by adding 80,000 square feet to introduce its HyForge technology for automotive billets and components. news_summary: Copper prices fell below $10,000 per tonne on the LME, down 1.5%, driven by weak demand expectations from China and persistently high Shanghai physical inventories. Softer U.S. data and rate-cut expectations supported a stronger dollar, adding near-term pressure. Aluminium and nickel also edged lower. | BEAC’s CEMAC report says mineral export prices fell 2.6% QoQ in March 2023, mainly due to weaker iron and manganese prices. It also notes that aluminum prices rose 2.7% during the same period, though not enough to offset declines across other commodities exported by the region.

news_summary: Copper prices fell after a strong rally as weak physical demand in China weighed on the market. LME three-month copper slipped below $10,000 and SHFE July copper dropped, while SHFE and LME copper stocks rose. Aluminium and nickel also eased, suggesting broadly bearish sentiment in base metals. | Copper prices near a one-month low as real demand fails to match strong electrification-driven expectations. Three-month LME copper slipped below $10,000, with sellers paying China to destock. Funds reportedly sold copper and bought aluminium. Aluminium was also down on the day; nickel fell alongside broader metals weakness. | Russian aluminium producer Rusal is in talks with Sierra Leone’s government about securing a bauxite mining concession. The company says it wants to strengthen its raw material base as supply chain disruptions and refinery issues have pushed it to seek more alumina. Rusal already operates in Guinea. | Germany’s aluminium industry saw Q1 2024 production declines across subsectors, with recycled and semi-finished output down and extrusion hit by rising third-country imports, especially Turkey. High energy costs and weak end-demand (including construction and EV funding cancellations) worsen pressure, suggesting softer near-term aluminium pricing. | Chinese researchers say they developed nanoparticles-enhanced, oxide-dispersion-strengthened aluminum alloys with markedly improved heat resistance beyond typical performance limits. The work, presented as scalable and important for aerospace use, could broaden aluminum’s high-temperature applications. The piece also discusses China’s shift toward greener electricity for aluminum smelting.

news_summary: Copper futures rebounded above $10,000/t as the dollar softened on expectations of Fed easing and as signs of firmer China demand emerged. LME three-month copper rose 1.6%. LME aluminum and nickel also advanced, with Reuters citing renewed copper buying activity in China. | Silver One says it found additional copper and silver showings at its Phoenix Silver Project in Arizona, with select samples up to 7.79% Cu. The area is near Freeport McMoRan’s Miami copper mine and BHP’s Ocelot porphyry project, and drilling is targeted for Q3 2024. | Copper prices rebounded 1% to around $10,027/ton ahead of the ECB’s expected first rate cut, supported by optimism that lower rates may improve growth. Gains were capped by concerns over sluggish demand, especially in China where inventories rose and producer operating rates fell. LME aluminium and nickel also rose.

LME aluminium was nearly flat and nickel edged up slightly. | Vedanta’s quarterly update said aluminium production rose about 3% year-on-year to 595,000 tonnes in the June quarter. news_summary: DHS published an updated UFLPA strategy identifying new high-priority enforcement sectors, including aluminum. The policy increases scrutiny of imports linked to forced labor in China, potentially causing delays, refusals, or higher compliance costs for aluminum shipments, which may disrupt near-term supply expectations for aluminum markets. | The Biden administration introduced 10% tariffs on aluminium imported from Mexico when it was not smelted in Mexico, the US, or Canada, alongside 25% on steel. The measure targets Chinese-origin metal routed via Mexico. Because a portion of Mexican aluminium is smelted outside North America, supply to the US may tighten. | The US imposed “symbolic” tariffs on steel and aluminum products originating from China via Mexico, closing a loophole that allowed Chinese suppliers to evade US tariffs since 2018. Aluminum imports routed this way face higher tariff costs, likely dampening demand and adding near-term bearish pressure to aluminium prices. | Mexico has introduced 20%–35% import duties on steel, aluminum sheets/coils, and ferroalloys, with exemptions for partners in Mexico’s free trade agreements. The piece highlights potential trade tensions and reduced global market stability. The aluminum tariffs are the most direct driver potentially pressuring aluminum prices in coming days. | The US issued Section 232 proclamations for aluminum imports from Mexico, replacing prior tariff exclusions with “country of smelt” and “most recent cast” rules. If smelt/cast is linked to China, Russia, Belarus, or Iran, the 10% tariff is reinstated and the 200% Russia-related tariff can apply, effective immediately. | Biden’s July 10 orders introduce stricter certification and smelt/cast origin requirements for U.S. aluminum imports from Mexico to qualify for Section 232 tariff exemptions. Imports containing aluminum smelt/cast in China, Russia, Belarus, or Iran face higher duties, aimed at curbing transshipment and excess supply.

news_summary: London copper fell 0.3% as the dollar firmed, reflecting reduced appeal for non-USD buyers and concerns over China demand amid high inventories. Investors awaited China GDP, retail sales and industrial output data. In parallel, LME aluminium slipped slightly and nickel edged higher, indicating mixed, limited FX-driven pressure. | China’s primary aluminium output rose 6.2% in June to 3.67 million tonnes as producers increased runs on higher profits. Inner Mongolia added capacity and Yunnan resumed output supported by summer hydropower. Reuters cites record monthly production and higher industry margins, implying near-term supply pressure on aluminium prices. | Copper steadied as softer China data increased bets for additional stimulus ahead of a key Communist Party meeting. While surging inventories and strong production capped gains, investors still expected improved demand. The piece also shows mild mixed moves in aluminium and nickel alongside copper as the dollar firmed. | Constellium said it completed the first industrial-scale hydrogen casting at its R&D center, producing a 12-ton aluminium slab with no observed quality impact. The hydrogen method replaces natural gas in casting and supports decarbonization efforts, with the slab slated for EV-related aluminium processing in France. | Copper prices fell as weak Chinese economic data and higher LME-approved inventories weighed on demand expectations. LME stocks rose to the highest since Oct 2021, and a firmer dollar added pressure. The article also highlights weaker aluminium pricing tied to higher China primary output and reports nickel down. | Osisko Gold Royalties and Franco-Nevada will invest $100 million initially and $650 million later to fund pre-construction and construction costs for SolGold’s Cascabel copper-gold project in Ecuador. The prefeasibility study projects multi-decade copper and gold output, but timing is future, limiting near-term price impact. | Portugal’s environment and energy minister said a new strategic plan for green-transition critical raw materials may give copper a more important role than lithium, highlighting copper’s relevance for electric vehicles. Portugal already has the EU’s largest copper mine and may pursue new concessions, supporting near-term copper supply expectations. | Nornickel says it will restart a furnace at the Nadezhda Smelter in early August after Rbs30bn repairs delayed by equipment sourcing problems under Western sanctions. The smelter is critical for refined nickel output. Separate LME and US/UK measures continue restricting Russian copper, aluminium and nickel flows. | Putin said Russia became a top producer of high-grade nickel and a leading producer of aluminum last year, citing expert estimates. The statement frames ongoing development and advanced alloy production, but provides no immediate policy or operational changes that would clearly shift near-term nickel or aluminum supply expectations. | Rio Tinto’s Q2 results show mined copper production at 171 kt, up 18% year-on-year, with Oyu Tolgoi underground ramp-up and higher head grades supporting output. Aluminium output was slightly higher (824 kt) and Rio Tinto secured long-term renewable power for New Zealand’s Tiwai smelter, supporting continuity of supply.

news_summary: Aluminium prices fell to a three-month low as primary output rose in major producing countries, with Shanghai LME inventories climbing to the highest since April 2023. Shanghai nickel also hit a four-month trough on increased production, including Indonesia’s output, pressuring an over-supplied market. | China’s unwrought aluminium imports rose 16% year-on-year in June to 240,000 tonnes, with first-half imports up sharply. Bauxite imports also increased 15.4% in June. Strong inflows of primary metal and feedstock suggest higher near-term supply, potentially pressuring aluminium prices. | Aluminium prices hit a three-month low on rising primary output in major producing countries, particularly China, and higher SHFE inventories. Shanghai nickel also fell to a four-month trough as supply increased, including from Indonesia, contributing to an over-supplied market and downward pressure across short-term contracts. | India’s Material Recycling Association (MRAI) urges the government to remove the 2.5% import duty on aluminium scrap, arguing recycling cuts carbon emissions versus smelting. The group asks for duty-free imports until enough quality domestic scrap is available, potentially improving scrap supply for aluminium recyclers. | Anglo American said its Q2 production dipped for copper, but it kept 2024 copper guidance unchanged, citing Chile and Peru throughput and unit cost levels. Nickel production was broadly flat with unchanged 2024 guidance and lower unit costs. Aluminium was not discussed, and no new disruption was announced. | Japan buyers agreed to pay a higher Q3 aluminum premium of $172/mt, up 16%-19% from the prior quarter, citing tighter regional supply as some metal was diverted to Europe and the US. Negotiations ended below initial offers, while Japanese demand stayed lackluster and port stocks rose modestly. | Indonesia will launch SIMBARA, an online tracking system for nickel and tin shipments, next week. The system links smelters’ purchases to mine registration and bars unloading ore from mines that have not paid royalties. It is tied to RKAB quota records and aims to boost revenue and improve mining governance. | Copper prices fell toward three-week lows as China offered no new stimulus, with Beijing communique lacking details, and Chinese copper imports slipping to a 14-month low. LME copper inventories rose to a 33-month high, while investors rotated into gold on expectations of Fed rate cuts. Aluminium also hit a recent low. | Peru’s copper production growth is forecast to slow to 0.6% in 2024 due to mining disruptions from blockades and unfavorable weather. Las Bambas was blocked until April 11 and produced less in Q1. Despite this, overall Peru copper output rose 3.6% YoY in Q1 on other mines’ gains. | Biden issued proclamations refining Section 232 tariff exclusions for aluminum imports from Mexico. New “country of smelt” and “most recent cast” requirements require certificates of analysis to prove primary aluminum is not from China, Russia, Belarus, or Iran for duty-free treatment, with tariffs reinstated otherwise.

news_summary: Copper hit a more than three-month low after China’s political meeting failed to deliver further stimulus measures, reducing expectations for metal demand. Weekly losses widened. Aluminium also fell to multi-month lows amid rising output, and nickel reached a four-month low, reflecting increased supply and weaker sentiment. | SMM reported social inventory of aluminum ingots rose to 797,000 mt in H2, including 671,000 mt available for sale, lifting supply and suppressing spot and futures. Inventory increased mid-week and reached a new high, reflecting weaker northwest processing demand and reduced shipments to south China. | Copper prices fell for a fifth session to a three-month low as markets stayed bearish on China’s weak economy and the absence of additional stimulus. SHFE copper also dropped. Aluminium and nickel hit multi-month lows, weighed by higher output in Indonesia and China, reinforcing overall downside momentum. | Alcoa’s CEO says the Alumina Ltd acquisition is on track to close August 1 and will simplify the company’s structure. The deal timing is influenced by elevated alumina prices amid supply disruptions, including refinery curtailments, gas pipeline problems affecting cargoes, and issues at Nalco, supporting aluminium cost expectations. | Aluminum industry associations from the US, Europe, Canada and Japan back an OECD effort to quantify subsidies and state-enterprise roles. The report argues Chinese aluminium firms receive most subsidies and below-market energy/finance. Associations urge governments to apply trade defence measures for a fair, level playing field in aluminium markets. | An EPA lawsuit alleges air quality violations at the Ferndale aluminum plant. Enforcement actions such as mandated corrective measures, operating restrictions, or potential downtime could affect near-term aluminum output, which would likely be bearish for aluminium price expectations over the coming days to weeks.

news_summary: Norwegian aluminium producer Norsk Hydro said Q2 core profit fell 18% to 5.84 billion crowns, missing analysts’ expectations. The decline was attributed to lower volumes and margins, even though alumina prices were higher. The result suggests weaker operating conditions for aluminium markets near term. | Indonesia plans to broaden its Simbara online mineral tracking system to additional commodities, following Monday’s inclusion of nickel and tin. The minister said copper and bauxite (plus manganese and others) will follow. Officials cite state revenue gains from preventing illegal mining, but no immediate production change is specified. | Indonesia plans to expand its SIMBARA online tracking system for minerals and coal to cover additional commodities including copper, after it was broadened to nickel and tin. The system targets illegal mining through risk profiling and automatic blocking, contributing state revenues. No immediate production or aluminium-specific impact is detailed. | Indonesia’s energy ministry plans to extend its Simbara online tracking system to additional commodities. It already broadened Simbara to cover nickel and tin and now intends to add copper, bauxite, manganese and others. Simbara helps curb illegal mining via risk profiling and automatic blocking, contributing to state revenues. | Porsche lowered its annual forecast after aluminum-alloy supply bottlenecks. A flooding incident at an important European supplier triggered force majeure, disrupting production of special aluminium alloys used across Porsche vehicle series. Porsche expects impairment for several weeks, potentially delaying model-series output and deliveries, which can reduce short-term aluminium demand. | Porsche’s shares fell after it cut its 2024 outlook, blaming a significant shortage of special aluminium alloys. The shortage is linked to flooding at a key European aluminium supplier’s facility, disrupting aluminium body components across vehicle series. Porsche expects production impairments for several weeks and possible series shutdowns. | Copper prices fell for a seventh straight session to the lowest in three and a half months as fears of softer Chinese demand grew. Reuters cited disappointment over China’s third plenum, sell signals from trading models, record Chinese shipments raising LME stocks, and a wider refined copper surplus, weighing on sentiment. Aluminium bucked the trend. | Reuters reports that BMW experienced aluminium shortages that affected production, but the company had contingency plans. BMW said there was no impact on overall output or its outlook. The news signals temporary aluminium supply tightness affecting manufacturers, but with limited broader demand implications in the near term. | BMW said an aluminium alloy supply shortage also affected its supply chain, but it compensated for the lost supply and production was not interrupted. The shortage previously influenced Porsche’s outlook, implying some stress in aluminium alloy availability, but BMW’s mitigation limits immediate additional upside risk for aluminium prices. | Speira is investing €11m to upgrade two German aluminium recycling plants with tiltable rotary furnaces capable of processing heavily contaminated low-grade scrap and dross. The upgrades include automatic charging and more efficient burner/afterburner systems, aiming for higher aluminium yield and lower energy use, with production targeted from May 2025 onward. | Novelis notified automotive customers of a force majeure that led it to shut a Swiss aluminium plant after late-June flooding. The disruption meant it could not fully deliver aluminium supplies in Europe. Porsche also cut its sales forecast after another European aluminium supplier declared force majeure. | Sucden Financial’s head of research says copper’s recent upside was driven mainly by re-engaged speculative long positions, with markets watching COMEX/LME arbitrage. Copper strength is expected to lift the wider base-metals complex, with aluminium following closely, while nickel may diverge due to Indonesia supply and inventory growth.

Physical demand stayed weak with high inventories, pressuring also nickel and aluminium. | Two Las Vegas public works workers were critically injured replacing stolen copper lines. Weak physical demand and high inventories were cited, alongside equity losses. Nickel and aluminium also fell. | Brixton Metals reported initial 2024 drill assay results from its Thorn Project, highlighting long broad copper-dominant porphyry intervals with associated Au, Ag and Mo, and noting mineralization remains open at depth. news_summary: Copper extended its third straight drop on concerns about demand in top consumer China and uncertainty over when interest-rate cuts will arrive. Chinese stimulus aimed more at consumers than infrastructure and property weakness weighed. The piece also reports aluminium fell as risk-off sentiment dragged industrial metals. | Aluminum prices have continued to fall on both the LME and China’s SHFE since early June, despite earlier bullish hopes tied to EVs, renewables, and grid spending. The decline is linked to weak Chinese demand, sluggish Europe/U.S. activity, rising inventories, record Chinese production, and high Russian/cheap imports creating oversupply. | Cameroon signed a bauxite mining agreement worth at least $2 billion with a subsidiary of Canyon Resources for the Minim-Martap project. The plan includes producing alumina from bauxite, transporting via existing rail to ports, and exporting the output. Mining is projected to begin in about two years.

Results increase confidence in continuity of historical zones. The release is exploratory and does not mention production, shipments, or new supply disruptions affecting prices. | Canyon Resources signed a mining convention for its Minim-Martap bauxite project in Cameroon, contingent on a mining permit, with government officials present. news_summary: The news is a market research outlook for the aluminium curtain wall industry, forecasting growth from USD 43 billion (2023) to USD 92.83 billion by 2033. It cites infrastructure spending, urbanisation, and technology upgrades driving construction demand, which could support aluminium demand and prices modestly. | U.S. DOJ fined the closed Intalco aluminum smelting plant $5.25 million for repeated Clean Air Act violations, including illegal emissions monitoring and heavy-metal releases. Powell’s rate-cut hint offered limited offset. | India’s Ministry of Mines said primary aluminium output rose 1.2% in Q1 FY25 to 10.43 lakh tonnes versus 10.28 lakh tonnes a year earlier. The government also cited robust growth in iron ore and limestone. Continued aluminium production growth supports expectations of steady near-term supply.

news_summary: London copper rose 0.4% but was set for a flat week as investors await US jobs data while China’s demand outlook remains downbeat. Copper inventories stay high. LME aluminium edged down. Nickel prices were supported by tight Indonesian ore supply, but overall the week’s direction appears muted. | China’s state planner set renewable-energy targets for aluminium production in key provinces. news_summary: London copper edged up 0.2% after prior sell-offs, as some dip-buying emerged, but global demand concerns capped upside. The U.S. jobs report and weaker China activity fueled recession worries while expectations of Fed cuts supported industrial demand. LME aluminium and nickel rose slightly, showing mixed sentiment. | Copper prices tumbled as worsening China and U.S. demand outlooks sparked a sell-off. Weaker U.S. jobs data and technology earnings reinforced recession fears, while rising LME warehouse inventories weighed near term. The risk-off move also pushed aluminium and nickel modestly lower alongside copper. | Copper fell sharply (up to 3.8% on the LME) as global financial contagion triggered a risk-off rush to cash out and turn bearish amid fears of a US recession. The selloff pressured other industrial raw-materials, reflecting weaker growth expectations and tighter financial conditions.

news_summary: Copper edged up slightly on a softer U.S. dollar, but broader sentiment is weak as LME inventories jumped and global growth expectations deteriorated. Commentary notes rising TC/RCs suggest improving concentrate supply. Aluminium and nickel prices were also lower in the same session, indicating mixed but generally pressured industrial metals. | Copper prices steadied but remained under pressure as pessimism on global growth and risk-off sentiment weighed on base metals. LME copper inventories surged nearly threefold to 294,750 tons. Aluminium and nickel also fell on the day, reflecting weaker demand expectations outweighing improving spot refining charges. | Golden Arrow reported new step-out drill intercepts at its San Pietro IOCG project in Chile, including long intervals grading about 0.43–0.44% Cu with associated gold and cobalt. The release is an exploration update with no production or reserve change stated, so any near-term effect on copper prices is likely limited. | Traders expect improved Japanese automotive demand to support aluminium in the latter half of 2024 after Toyota certification and recall-related production suspensions. Toyota models resume in September following compliance confirmation, while Japan Aluminium Association data shows aluminium usage in transportation. Fastmarkets also moves the Japan spot premium publication to daily to improve liquidity. | Canadian steel and aluminum producers are urging Ottawa to impose tariffs on Chinese steel and aluminum, arguing current measures have not stopped dumping and market overcapacity. The push follows recent U.S. tariff increases on Chinese metals and concerns Canada could become a trade backdoor via Mexico, risking job losses and potential price pressure in aluminum markets. | Aluminum producers are pressing Canada’s Trudeau government to act on China-related tariffs. The move would likely raise the cost of importing Chinese aluminum into Canada, potentially reducing near-term supply and supporting domestic prices as markets price in a stronger tariff-driven import barrier. | Meviy (MISUMI Group) said it will cut the sales price of its CNC milling aluminum products by 30% effective immediately, covering aluminum alloys 6061, 5052, and 2017 with no surface treatment. The move targets lower customer costs and competitive pricing, likely signaling local pricing pressure on aluminium products rather than metal prices broadly.
