news_summary: Mitsui underwrites a capital increase in Nippon Amazon Aluminium (NAAC), raising its stake in the Albras smelter joint venture to about 46% (direct and indirect). The deal also increases annual offtake of primary aluminium ingots from ~80,000 to ~140,000 tons, strengthening green aluminium supply access. | Canadian steel and aluminum CEOs urge Ottawa to impose tariffs on China to prevent North America from becoming a “dumping ground” for Chinese overcapacity. They seek trade protection aligned with the U.S. and Mexico. If implemented, reduced Chinese import pressure could support aluminium pricing in the near term. | Copper rose Friday after U.S. jobless claims came in better than expected, easing recession-demand fears and supporting rate-cut optimism. It still faces a fifth straight weekly drop, with LME inventories at a high since 2019 limiting upside catalysts. Aluminium and nickel also climbed amid the broader rebound. | Mitsui increased its stake in Nippon Amazon Aluminium (NAAC) to expand purchases of low-carbon aluminum ingots from Brazil’s Albras. Offtake rises to 140,000 tonnes/year from 80,000 tonnes/year, mainly for Japanese buyers. NAAC/Albras use renewable power, and Mitsui expects tight low-carbon supply and rising demand. | Ormonde notes Peak Nickel has resumed a 2024 drill programme at its Rodburn Ni-Cu-Co project in Scotland, targeting further definition and potential expansion of mineralization. The announcement includes Ni, Cu and Co grades used in Ni-equivalent assumptions, but provides no new price-driving results or production impacts. | Pierre Poilievre said he would implement tariffs on Chinese exports, including aluminum, alongside electric vehicles and steel, citing protection of Canadian jobs from “economic predation” by Beijing. He made the remarks at the Stelco steel plant, arguing tariffs would encourage new mining and steel investment. | Technavio projects growth in the coal tar pitch market from 2024–2028, citing increasing use in primary aluminum production. Coal tar pitch is used as a binder/anode component in the Hall-Héroult process and electrode demand. Regulations limiting coal tar pitch due to PAHs are noted as a constraint, limiting clear short-term aluminum price impact.

news_summary: Reuters reports copper prices fell as LME inventories climbed to near five-year highs and China demand remained sluggish. Traders cited exports of surplus copper into Asian LME warehouses and weaker Chinese macro data. Purchases from some physical consumers and hopes of a BHP Escondida strike limited losses, but overall sentiment stayed bearish. | The U.S. issued proclamations modifying Section 232 trade treatment for certain steel and aluminum products from Mexico. For aluminum, importers must document smelt/cast countries; otherwise an additional 10% duty applies. This raises costs for some Mexico-origin aluminum shipments, likely reducing near-term demand and weighing on aluminum prices.

news_summary: Emirates Global Aluminium (EGA) says it has shipped bauxite from Guinea to China on an LNG-fuelled, dual-fuel capesize vessel, aiming to cut transport emissions versus traditional marine fuel. Bauxite is the feedstock for aluminium, but the news does not indicate changes to bauxite volumes or aluminium production schedules.

news_summary: Rio Tinto signed an agreement with Queensland to safeguard its Boyne aluminum smelter in Gladstone, backed by the state Energy and Jobs Plan as the plant shifts to renewable power. Queensland support from 2029 and Rio’s investment to keep capacity above 500,000 tonnes, plus more bauxite shipping, bolster aluminum supply expectations. | China’s July primary aluminium output rose 6% to 3.68m tonnes, the highest monthly total in over 20 years, as smelters ramped up due to still-profitable operations. While prices recently fell and demand stayed weak, analysts expect profits to remain steady and output to rise further, weighing on aluminium sentiment. | Nevada’s NDOT restored streetlights on US 95 after theft caused outages; officials said stolen copper wiring was removed and the repairs were bundled into ongoing road work. Crews also added protective lids and security measures. The new LED lighting is more energy efficient and requires less maintenance, but the impact appears localized. | Queensland partnered with Rio Tinto to secure the future of the Boyne/B2L aluminium smelter (BSL) and support renewable energy and grid upgrades. news_summary: Thailand’s Department of Foreign Trade opened a nine-month anti-dumping investigation into Chinese extruded aluminium after local producers complained of injury from imports. Improved volumes and rerouting are mildly supportive for prices. | Rusal’s first-half adjusted net profit rose 41.6% as it increased production and shipped record volumes to China. The firm faced demand pressure as Western buyers avoid Russia-made metal after Ukraine. The article notes April LME bans on Russian aluminium, copper and nickel and links improved output to higher prices for aluminium and alumina. | Corella Resources discusses government funding for R&D aimed at improving high-purity alumina (HPA) flowsheeting from kaolin, including reducing high-temperature calcination to lower energy intensity. The work is expected to take about six months, but the update is primarily technical and early-stage, with no near-term supply change stated. | WA EPA has set a deadline for Alcoa to submit information required for bauxite mining approvals in the jarrah forest, warning it could take a cautious approach or impose stricter conditions if information is not provided. Water and environmental agencies opposed the plans, citing risks to drinking water and ecosystems. | Queensland is considering an extra $1.2 billion for the CopperString high-voltage transmission line after costs rose to an estimated $6.2 billion. The project is expected to support mining development and help supply cheaper power, likely boosting near-term expectations for grid metals, especially copper, with possible spillover to aluminium. | An explosion at an aluminium smelter in Inner Mongolia disrupted three aluminium electrolytic cells. The company resolved the issue quickly, and the affected tanks resumed production that afternoon. The update characterizes the production disruption as minimal, suggesting limited near-term impact on aluminium supply expectations. | The article reports that aluminium is leading base metals lower as market conditions point to a weaker supply-and-demand balance. This suggests reduced support from physical or demand fundamentals, contributing to downward price momentum in aluminium relative to other base metals over the near term. | Argentina’s minister says the pro-business mining incentive regime (RIGI) should attract major foreign investment and help new lithium and copper projects start later this year. Copper is framed as crucial for EVs and AI, with large capex needs for projects, implying improved near-term supply/export expectations. | Times of Oman reports Omani project “Greenlium,” using lignin extracted from palm waste to convert commercial aluminum into a more sustainable “organic” aluminum. It claims lower environmental impact and potential applications in packaging and electronics. The article does not indicate commercial scale or near-term supply/cost changes for aluminum prices. | US steel and aluminium groups and the UAW urged the USTR to expand Section 301 tariff rates, including proposals to target third-country imports that incorporate Chinese-origin aluminium. The Aluminum Association argues current trade diversion undermines US producers, seeking added tariff coverage. USTR reviews comments for a final decision in August.

news_summary: Canada’s Department of Finance announced 100% tariffs on Chinese-made EVs and a 25% tariff on certain steel and aluminum products from China, with measures starting in October. The aluminum tariff can tighten supply from China into Canada, offering near-term support to aluminium prices. | The Overnight Report links overnight weakness in industrial metals to a stronger USD and specific aluminium factors. It says aluminium led metals lower as worries persist over China’s demand recovery, while ample supply grew. Rusal’s record first-half aluminium shipments lifted China inventories to their highest levels since 2019. | Unite union workers at Alvance Aluminium’s Lochaber smelter near Fort William agreed a two-year pay rise above inflation, including 5%/4% increases in 2024 and additional scheduled raises in 2025. The deal covers smelter and administrative staff and follows no reported production disruption; a recycling and billet project is planned. | U.S. Trade Representative Katherine Tai said Canada’s new high tariffs on some Chinese imports are an important step for fair competition in electric vehicles and the steel and aluminum industries. By targeting Chinese aluminum imports, the measure is likely to tighten supply and support near-term aluminum prices.

news_summary: Tindo partnered with Capral Aluminium to supply extruded aluminium frames for Australian-made solar panels, with a pathway to sourcing from Capral’s domestic facilities as Tindo scales output toward 1GW. The collaboration is aimed at strengthening Australia’s PV supply chain and could increase near-term aluminium demand.

news_summary: The White House issued updated Section 232 proclamations covering aluminum imports. New “smelt and most recent cast” origin rules for Mexico-linked aluminum and higher duties apply when smelt/cast is reported in China or other listed countries, raising compliance costs and reducing duty-free inflows into the US. | US steel and aluminum groups and the Aluminum Association urged USTR to expand proposed Section 301 tariff rates to cover Chinese-origin aluminium embedded in third-country products re-exported to the US. AISI also sought broader steel rules of origin. USTR will finalize changes in August, taking effect shortly after publication. | Alpha HPA began stage two construction at its Gladstone, Queensland high-purity alumina facility, building on stage one and planning over 10,000 tonnes annually of high-purity aluminium products. The expansion is funded with government debt and additional equity and targets electronics for the energy transition, supporting aluminium supply expectations. | Fastmarkets launched new FOB “alumina index inferred” assessments for Indonesia (and also Vietnam/India), reflecting rising Indonesian influence. The piece links Indonesia’s growing alumina output to capacity constraints in Australia, where refinery outages and force majeure have disrupted alumina supply.

news_summary: ADEM and Ecovery entered a consent order over alleged air-permit violations at an Alabama secondary aluminum recycling facility. The plant processes aluminum-copper radiators and had visible particulate emissions linked to south dust collector maintenance. The firm replaced filters and seals; later stack tests showed compliance. A $15,000 civil penalty was assessed. | European aluminium premiums in Rotterdam and Italy remain well-supported, with the duty-unpaid premium edging higher amid tight regional supply, concerns over unpaid stock safety, low inventories, and high costs to source replacements. Japanese spot premiums narrowed on bearish sentiment and contango dynamics, while US activity stayed sluggish.

news_summary: SMM data show China’s August 2024 aluminum output rose 1.8% YoY to 3.69 Mt, supported by new capacity in Inner Mongolia and little disruption from Sichuan power rationing. Operating rate reached 95.88%. September output is forecast stable near 3.57 Mt amid improving downstream billet activity. | An August 2024 wrap-up for aluminium highlights a moderate price increase on LME and SHFE, attributed to supply chain constraints and selective demand strength, despite volatility from geopolitics and weaker European foil deliveries. It also covers power/production factors (China output, renewable power caps) and supply chain initiatives affecting aluminium costs. | Kyrgyzstan has increased aluminium exports to China fourfold. The report indicates a large jump in outbound shipments, which can raise near-term supply availability in China. In the absence of offsetting demand or cost factors, such export growth is generally associated with downward pressure on aluminium prices.

news_summary: ARM says weaker platinum group metals and coal prices cut its annual profit, prompting a pivot toward chrome and copper. It approved a chrome recovery plant at Bokoni and acquired a 15% stake in Surge Copper. South African miners also eye copper assets, but timing to production is long-dated. | China filed a WTO complaint against Canada over Canadian tariffs on Chinese EVs plus 25% tariffs on steel and aluminium. Canada’s measures take effect next month and are aimed at unfair pricing and environmental/labour standards. China signals retaliation risk, adding near-term uncertainty for industrial demand. | China has filed a WTO complaint against Canada over Canada’s new tariffs on Chinese electric vehicles and 25% tariffs on steel and aluminum, with Canada’s measures set to start next month. Analysts warn retaliation could raise costs and pressure markets, adding near-term uncertainty for aluminum supply and prices. | Fairfax India will invest about $83 million for a 65% stake in Hyderabad-based Global Aluminium, a major Indian aluminium extrusions producer with anodized and powder-coated product capabilities. The transaction is expected to close in Q4 2024 and may modestly influence near-term aluminium-related demand, but no major market supply effects are specified.
